programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan is accused of murdering his accountant after he is discovered leaning over the dead body. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke, Scott Baio and Victoria Rowell

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A charity chairman dies mysteriously, and the prime suspect is then killed at the funeral. The investigation leads to hospital administrator Norman Briggs - who could either be the guilty party or the next victim. Dick Van Dyke stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A womanising patient disappears and Dr Sloan finds himself trying to untangle an ambitious murder plot. Crime drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team learns the hackers who took control of US naval destroyers are also controlling a nuclear submarine. Walter faces the reality that he is not emotionally ready for Paige

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team rushes to return Walter to Earth when he is accidentally launched into space, where he hallucinates about Paige as his oxygen runs low. Drama, starring Elyes Gabel

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Homeland Security employee Victor Sterling mysteriously disappears and his car is found at a drive-through restaurant that becomes the scene of a double murder. To complicate matters further, the missing man's outspoken spouse happens to be the ex-wife of both Gibbs and Fornell. American naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, Joe Spano, Tom Gallop and Melinda McGraw

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. A military aircraft transporting the bodies of dead US marines back home crashes before reaching its destination, and Gibbs and the team are called in to help identify human remains at the site of the wreckage. However, a DNA discrepancy turns a routine investigation into a search for a missing person. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Ziva and Gibbs lead the mission to rescue the captured Marine in Afghanistan before discovering that her captor's brothers have bigger plans and are at large in America. Gibbs has to be stopped when he tries to employ methods similar to those used on Kinah and Lala in an effort to gain information, but Vance has a trick that gives the agents an idea of what the siblings have in mind

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony is forced to tackle a case with a personal element when his father is discovered drunk in a car that has a dead body in the boot. The victim is revealed to be Navy lieutenant Dean Massey, who DiNozzo Sr had threatened to kill after being cut out of a land development deal. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Navy captain Jake Marsden is murdered in his hotel room and inquiries lead to marine lieutenant Emma Reynolds - who tells the team she was the intended target. After being stationed in Kabul, she became pregnant by a local man called Amir who was later killed in action - and Abby discovers he was the sole male heir of a wealthy family that owns a large tract of land in a politically sensitive part of Afghanistan

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team learns the hackers who took control of US naval destroyers are also controlling a nuclear submarine. Walter faces the reality that he is not emotionally ready for Paige

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team rushes to return Walter to Earth when he is accidentally launched into space, where he hallucinates about Paige as his oxygen runs low. Drama, starring Elyes Gabel

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates when fellow agent Lara Macy's body is found badly burnt in a campfire on Annapolis beach. According to her laptop she was tailing an unco-operative rape victim, and the prime suspect in the crime is the son of a DC contractor. Gibbs knew Macy years ago, and he finds he can no longer avoid his Mexican past catching up with him as the case progresses. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Lt Dean captures Gibbs in Mexico and hands him over to Paloma Reynosa, head of a drug cartel and daughter of the man he killed. She tries to persuade him to aid her master plan by threatening to kill his father. Alejandro Rivera arrives with a platoon of Mexican troops, but hopes of rescue may be premature. Meanwhile, Ziva is accepted as a US citizen

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: Avery welcomes CSI's DB Russell to the team as they investigate a burglar and murder committed by someone who hacked the home's security system remotely using a computer chip hidden in a child's doll. Elsewhere, Elijah deals with a delicate family situation, and Krumitz learns that his sister Francine is facing a murder charge. Patricia Arquette and Ted Danson star

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: Raven's friend Tracy is fired, evicted and has her bank accounts emptied as a result of being hacked by a man she met on a dating app, so Raven helps to locate the culprit

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: When a police officer's body camera is hacked and the incendiary video goes viral, the Cyber team is sent to find the truth - and the hacker - before the town erupts in unrest. Crime drama, starring Patricia Arquette, James Van Der Beek and Ted Danson

Dick Van Dyke stars as the medical sleuth

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A TV star is caught on camera fleeing the scene of her former husband's murder, but Dr Sloan suspects she may have been framed. Guest starring Morgan Fairchild

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A valued member of staff at the hospital is suspected of killing a vindictive media magnate who was receiving treatment. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Scott Baio

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team is called to catch thieves who have stolen a rare gem from LA's Natural History Museum, in an attempt extract materials required to enrich a nuclear bomb

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The NCIS team is assigned to a joint terrorism task force by the Secretary when a Moscow-born scientist who it is believed may have been targeted by the Russians for selling state secrets, is found dead. The trail appears to lead to a friend of Sofia's, another scientist who worked for Russia and who may have succumbed to professional jealousy. However, when she too is found dead, the team discovers the hallmarks of Sergei Mishnev, the terrorist who nearly killed Gibbs. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While on a joint Coast Guard and NCIS training exercise at sea with Agent Borin, Gibbs spots a dead man overboard, who is found to be a missing member of the crew of a nearby vessel. However, when the agent boards the ship for questioning, he realises it has been commandeered by pirates. Naval drama, starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a Navy commander's wife, determining whether it is linked to her role as a therapist, or an act of terrorism since her husband's name has appeared on a Jihadist's target list. Elsewhere, the group discusses Halloween costumes, as well as Tony's recent dating habits. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team uncovers a fraudulent charity that preys on those searching for missing military personnel while investigating the murder of a retired Master Sergeant. Meanwhile, Bishop agonises over the results of her probation evaluation, and Tony contemplates adopting a stray cat that followed him home. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the cause of a hit-and-run incident in which a Petty Officer has been killed. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: Feature-length drama in which the Middle Ages' answer to Sherlock Holmes sets out to investigate a slave girl's disappearance and locate a treasured relic missing from his abbey. Starring Derek Jacobi, Terrence Hardiman, Anthony Green, George Irving and Louise Delamere

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: The locals bid an emotional farewell to Vic and Lucy, and Simon's problems continue to mount as his business crumbles and he is consumed by guilt over his fling with Evelyn. Elsewhere, a dishy gardener steals the heart of a wheelchair-bound patient, while Tom and Beth take advantage of a drunken Joe's incapacity and steal a kiss. Rural drama, starring Thomas Craig and Tony Haygarth

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: The Skelthwaite Scorpions play a crunch match against rival team the Bridlington Trawlers, but fare badly, prompting David to demand a return engagement. Meanwhile, Anna's distress at her split with Simon is compounded by the financial hardship of running a business single-handed _ but just as disaster looms, a shady businessman makes her an offer for the factory. Lesley Dunlop and Philip Middlemiss star

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Richard Sharpe and the faithful Harper begin their latest tour of duty by uncovering top-level corruption in the Army. Reporting their findings leads to attempts on their lives, so they fake their own deaths and re-enlist under new identities in an attempt to expose the culprits. Guest starring Julian Fellowes and featuring Alexander Armstrong in an early role

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey's knight in shining armour arrives in the form of paroled rock legend Abel Kane, whisking them off on a winter break to Majorca, where yet more unexpected good fortune awaits them. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Marlene allows Boycie to buy a maximum of six cows for the farm - and is less than impressed when Elgin, Jed and Bryan turn up with a herd of 300. The vet is called out when Rocky the bull fails to take any notice of the females, and Tyler prepares to sit a local private school's entrance exam. John Challis and Sue Holderness star

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Boycie struggles to come to terms with the news that his prize bull is gay, and Marlene finds she is missing the noise of the city after suffering sleepless nights. Tyler is delighted to learn he has failed all but two of his GCSEs, thinking he will no longer be accepted at a private school - but his parents have other ideas. John Challis and Sue Holderness star

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Marlene discovers the farmhands concocting a virility potion for Rocky the bull, and manages to sneak a dose into Boycie's coffee. Make-up is found in Tyler's room, and his parents are relieved to be told he has a girlfriend - until it transpires she is also his teacher. John Challis, Sue Holderness and Jack Doolan star

programme.title: The Lady Vanishes
programme.desc: It is 1931 and socialite Iris Carr (Tuppence Middleton) is travelling back from a holiday in the Balkans, but faints on the platform of the railway station in the scorching heat. She is comforted by fellow passenger Miss Froy (Selina Cadell), but when Iris falls asleep she awakes to find the woman has disappeared and everyone else on board denies ever having seen her. With only handsome traveller Max Hare (Tom Hughes) for support, Iris maintains that Miss Froy has somehow been abducted - even though it increasingly appears to be a delusion on her part. Mystery, also starring Keeley Hawes, Gemma Jones, Stephanie Cole and Julian Rhind-Tutt. Adapted from Ethel Lina White's novel, The Wheel Spins

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Alzheimer sufferer Edward Alderston comes under suspicion of involvement when an ex-finance company high-flyer responsible for the loss of many elderly people's life savings is found floating in a swimming pool with a samurai sword in his back

programme.title: Father Brown

Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams

Meanwhile, there is a shock in store for Briggs and DI Sawyer when old foes and wrong-doings come back to haunt them. Police drama set in the 1950s, starring Claudia Jessie and Oliver Rix

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. McVitie suffers a heart attack and Jim is promoted to acting superintendent in his absence, but does not take kindly to the extra responsibility - or being accused of benefiting from someone else's misfortune. Crime drama, featuring the final appearance of Mark McManus as Jim Taggart

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: After fulfilling her dying aunt's last wishes, Jane returns to Thornfield Hall - only to find Mr Rochester is set to wed Blanche. However, after she confesses her love, he shares his true feelings and asks for her hand in marriage instead. While all seems well at first, a ghostly figure begins to torment Jane as the wedding day draws near. Drama, with Ruth Wilson and Toby Stephens

programme.desc: Third of an eight-part story. Diane and Nate investigate an attack on a doctor on the Jasmine Allen estate and discover his colleague Dr Rachel Cartwright (Josette Simon) has been performing illegal operations. Elsewhere, Carly Samuels' grieving mother Leanne is upset by a psychic's claim that she 'saw' Tito Morientes get into the car used in the drive-by shooting, but nobody is brave enough to give evidence leading to a prosecution

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Fourth of an eight-part story. Carly's funeral descends into chaos as the crowd chant 'murderer' at chief suspect Tito Morientes and a gunshot shatters the glass of the hearse. When order is restored, Leanne delivers a moving eulogy about her daughter which has a marked effect on Alisha, who eventually breaks down under further questioning and tells CID what she knows about the murder

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Fifth of an eight-part story. Tito Morientes is arrested on the testimony of Alisha Danniels for shooting Carly Samuels, but with Dwayne Fox still on the run the Sun Hill team face the problem of protecting their key witness. Fox's boss Marco Oliver is shocked at the killing and volunteers to help set up a sting operation to catch the second suspect before the rival gangs can get to Alisha

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part six of an eight-part story. The hit-and-run attack leaves Alisha Danniels too shaken and terrified to testify against Morientes and Fox, but she does identify the third man involved in Carly's death. Emma and Nate trace the vehicle and score a lucky bonus in identifying an ammunition seller who gives them the names of all the gun dealers on the Jasmine Allen estate

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Period medical drama chronicling the personal and professional consequences of Eleanor Bramwell's determined quest to become the first female surgeon at the East London Hospital. Jemma Redgrave stars

She is forced into a difficult decision that leaves Barbara facing the biggest test of her career. Nurse Crane meets a kindred spirit when she learns to speak Spanish, and Sister Evangelina helps a woman with breastfeeding, leading to unforeseen consequences

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A rare disease spreads around Poplar Street, and Patsy and Dr Turner try to trace the outbreak to its source. Suspicion over the identity of the first carrier threatens to divide a previously close-knit family. Sister Winifred is forced to confront her prejudices while treating a patient, and Barbara worries about how Trixie will react when she agrees to go to dinner with Tom

programme.title: The Murder Room
programme.desc: The first of a two-part drama by PD James. Love and hate form the basis of a case involving a family at war and a murder that appears to mimic the crimes displayed in a chilling private exhibition dedicated to gruesome real-life killings. Martin Shaw stars as Commander Adam Dalgliesh, with Janie Dee, Michael Maloney, Nicholas Le Prevost and Samantha Bond

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: During a heatwave in Brighton, the Lyell team is summoned to a waste dump where a body part has been found. The search is on to recover the other dismembered remains and piece together the victim's body, which is covered in intricate Japanese tattoos. Away from the lab, Nikki has some news that she desperately wants to share with Matt, but must wait until he visits from America

programme.desc: Hugh's estranged wife arrives from Las Vegas and creates chaos for Penny and his family. Charlie and Matt teach Hayley a lesson about love, lust and the need to forgive

programme.desc: The RMP begins a desperate search, as Tilbrook takes Markham to visit a very important prisoner and Ed receives intelligence that puts his life in danger. Alison confides in Honor about her affair as she finds herself unable to continue with her pregnancy

programme.desc: Donald and Ewan's fishing trip inadvertently brings a long-buried secret to light with the discovery of a lost German fighter plane in the loch. Isobel knows her godfather, local butcher Irvine Taylor, was the pilot and resolves to keep everyone else in the dark - but an MoD investigation threatens to scupper her plans. Elsewhere, Molly is devastated to learn she has a heart condition and a lonely Jess unwittingly agrees to take part in a karaoke competition with Ewan

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part seven of eight. Tension mounts at the start of the trial of Fox and Morientes for the murder of Carly Samuels and Grace is shocked to encounter an old flame in the courtroom. Smithy is furious to learn a key witness has retracted his statement and sets out with Emma to persuade Alisha Danniels to testify instead. Back at the station, a prisoner smashes his head against the wall and claims Nate attacked him

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Linden breaks up with Faye, but when she is threatened by a drug addict, his chivalrous attitude ends up placing him in grave danger. Meanwhile, Michael jeopardises his career by lashing out at Ric for ruining the Holbycare pitch, and Frieda is in a good mood when Oliver asks her out for drinks, little realising she has misread his friendly gesture

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Guppy is elated when he visits his father and they begin to settle their differences. However, his world soon crumbles when a shocking request from Jas leaves him in an impossible position. Rumours about Nina being promiscuous ruin her chances of a reconciliation with Abs, and Harry tries to crack the whip with the staff, whose festive spirit leaves him out in the cold

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Concluding the eight-part story. Smithy finally gets his witness to court to testify against Fox and Morientes after Tony rescues the crash victims from the overturned car. Defence barrister Sunil Davdra pulls Alisha's testimony apart but the case takes another surprising turn before a verdict is reached. On the Jasmine Allen estate, Beth and Reg find themselves barricaded in the mobile police unit as rioting breaks out

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Alan presses Frankie for an answer, Lorraine despairs at David's parenting skills, Joe makes a dramatic announcement, and Grant attempts a showdown with David

programme.desc: Alan's attempts to repair his marriage are thwarted by Carol's declaration that there's someone else in her life. Frankie plays games. David and Barry find the flat turned over

programme.desc: Jeremy announces he is planning to marry his girlfriend, but is surprised at his friends' response. Meanwhile, Cate Hobbs receives bad news

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Tristan has a mishap while treating a cow, leaving the Darrowby practice facing a possible lawsuit. Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison star

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Eric gets involved with an Irish rock band, while Lovejoy takes to the water with Lady Jane's friend Victoria Cavero and bravely pops the question - only to receive an unexpected response. Ian McShane, Dudley Sutton and Chris Jury star with guests Joanna Lumley and the members of Hothouse Flowers

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: The further romantic misadventures of the charming painter, played by Karl Howman. Jacko's interest in football is revived - much to Eric and Jean's amusement

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The store advertises on Citizen Band radio, but unfortunately Mrs Slocombe's attempt to publicise her underwear attracts the attentions of the wrong type of customer. Shop-floor comedy, with Mollie Sugden and featuring the voice of Robbie Coltrane

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Seymour departs for a new teaching job, leaving Clegg and Compo at a loose end now he is no longer around to drag them into his eccentric schemes. However, they do not realise an old friend is about to return to the village. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis, Bill Owen and Brian Wilde

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy makes an ill-advised attempt to establish long-distance communication with Compo and Clegg by investing in some walkie-talkies. However, it doesn't take long for one of the units to fall foul of a tractor. Long-running rural comedy, with Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: The detective is called out when a body is found on a rubbish pile under the Tyne Bridge. It initially looks like suicide, but evidence reveals the victim was dead before he landed. The search begins to uncover his identity, with just a pawnbroker's receipt, a note with a phone number for one Gwendolyn Howard and a betting slip in the deceased's pocket. Crime drama, guest starring Jim Moir (aka Vic Reeves)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: After Sandra's departure, the detectives are anxious to meet their new boss, and DCI Sasha Miller wastes no time getting cracking with her first Ucos case, the murder of an Italian immigrant on a west London allotment 25 years earlier. The ritualistic style of the killing puts one of the other gardeners in the frame when it's revealed he is a practising druid. However, as the investigation continues, Gerry becomes suspicious of Sasha - why is she kissing a senior officer? Tamzin Outhwaite joins the cast as DCI Miller, with Dennis Waterman, Denis Lawson and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Leo visits old college friend Sean Delaney at his psychiatric care centre in Essex, where one of the patients has been found dead in her bed. Although the coroner ruled a verdict of sudden-death syndrome, Delaney is not convinced and asks Leo to investigate. Suspicion soon falls on the dead girl's father, who has lost the family fortune, and his wife - but the pathologist runs into problems when the local detective sergeant makes it clear she is suspicious of his motives. programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v Wales (Kick-off 8.00pm). All the action from the match in the fifth round of fixtures, held at Stade de France. Les Bleus clinched a 27-23 victory when the sides met at Principality Stadium last year, but Wales prevailed 24-19 on their last visit here in 2019, and should head into this match in a confident mood after easily beating rivals England 40-24 in the third round of fixtures. With analysis from Sam Warburton, Martin Johnson and Thomas Castaignede, and commentary by Eddie Butler, Jonathan Davies and Benjamin Kayser

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents action from today's sixth-round ties - Bournemouth v Southampton at Vitality Stadium and Everton v Manchester City at Goodison Park. Bournemouth were the only Championship side remaining in the competition but will have been hoping to make home advantage count against their South Coast rivals, while the Citizens were looking to emulate their 3-1 away win over Everton in the league last month

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Brighton & Hove Albion v Newcastle United. Gary Lineker presents highlights of the Premier League match at Amex Stadium. Both teams were in need of points to boost their chances of avoiding relegation, and it was Brighton who were comfortable 3-0 victors when the sides met at St James' Park in September. Plus, a look back at Friday's clash between Fulham and Leeds United at Craven Cottage

programme.title: Bill Bailey: Limboland
programme.desc: The comedian muses on people's expectations of happiness in a performance recorded at the Hammersmith Apollo. programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Danny Dyer hosts the game show, which sees an engaged couple of teachers from Essex answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck. They hope to put the prize money towards a new home for themselves and their baby daughter

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Brighton & Hove Albion v Newcastle United. Gary Lineker presents highlights of the Premier League match at Amex Stadium. programme.desc: Stars of stage, screen and the movies gather to entertain the nation with hilarious sketches, live performances, big surprises and stunning music acts in a celebration to raise much-needed cash for good causes at home and across the world. Hosted by Lenny Henry, Davina McCall, Paddy McGuinness, Alesha Dixon and David Tennant

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Sheffield United (Kick-off 1.30pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the sixth-round tie at Stamford Bridge. The Blues are looking to reach the semi-finals for the fourth time in five seasons and enter this match as strong favourites given the struggles of the Blades in this season's Premier League. The sides have also met twice in the league this season, with Chelsea prevailing 4-1 here in November and 2-1 at Bramall Lane last month. With analysis from Micah Richards

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers compete to design a garden for a family of seven in Stamford, Lincolnshire, who want a peaceful garden that they can call their own

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Leicester City v Manchester United (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the sixth-round tie at King Power Stadium, as the line-up for the semi-finals is confirmed. United are unbeaten in their last 13 league and cup matches against Leicester, but the Foxes are enjoying a strong season so far and the sides shared the spoils in a 2-2 draw when they met here in the Premier League on Boxing Day. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Dion Dublin

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison celebrates the arrival of Spring on her home turf in the Cotswolds. From the millions of snowdrops in Painswick's Rococo Gardens to discovering some old folklore, finding out about a scheme to enable wheelchair users to enjoy tougher terrain in the great outdoors and meeting the cattle that encourage wildflowers, Ellie also raids the Countryfile Archive, revisiting the time she went on dolphin watch in Cardigan Bay, Matt Baker visited tulip fields in Lincolnshire, and Adam Henson went to North Yorkshire to catch his supper

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team takes some of greatest-ever cars featured in James Bond movies for a spin, including the iconic Aston Martin DB5. Freddie Flintoff gets behind the wheel of an Extreme E race car and puts it to the test against a man in a jet-pack, while Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness test a new Ferrari against a very old Alfa

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Jed Mercurio's smash-hit crime thriller returns for its sixth instalment, with Martin Compston, Vicky McClure and Adrian Dunbar reprising their roles as DS Steve Arnott, DI Kate Fleming and Superintendent Ted Hastings. New information comes to light about a possible suspect in a year-long unsolved murder, and DCI Joanne Davidson (Kelly Macdonald) appears keen to finally bring Gail Vella's killer to justice. However, her conduct soon arouses suspicion, and it's not long before AC-12 are on the case. But as their investigation uncovers deeper and darker information, will Davidson turn out to be their most dangerous adversary yet?

programme.desc: Action from the concluding sixth-round ties - Chelsea v Sheffield United at Bramall Lane and Leicester City v Manchester United at King Power Stadium

programme.title: A Time to Kill (1996)
programme.desc: A lawyer in Mississippi agrees to defend a black man charged with killing the two racist thugs who raped his 10-year-old daughter - but he is ill-prepared for the racial tension and violence that erupts during the course of the trial. Courtroom drama, based on a novel by John Grisham and starring Matthew McConaughey, Sandra Bullock, Samuel L Jackson, Donald Sutherland and Kevin Spacey

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the development of a garage in Margate, Kent, a ground-floor in London's Shepherd's Bush and a piece of land in Birmingham

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley are live, asking for your help with unsolved cases

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Paramedics treat a patient in the Glasgow Airport operations tower with a history of heart attacks, and a trauma team arrive by helicopter at a high speed car crash in Forth Valley

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents the show from Newark, Nottinghamshire, with experts Nick Hall and Jonathan Pratt. Featuring a look at the profitable field of police memorabilia

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Emma and Zara host a menopausal support group and learn as much about their own experiences and friendship as the other women

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple from London find a new home in Wiltshire with room for some animals. She also transforms a fossil into a piece of stylish jewellery

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Chefs Ellis Barrie and Jeremy Pang prepare meals against the clock, using ingredients brought in by two friends who teach young people life skills. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team set to work restoring a precious Indian stringed instrument, a Second World War pilot's jacket and a broken toy bird that has lost its tweet

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A year on from the first UK lockdown, Jane Corbin investigates Covid-19 policies pursued by governments around the world, and sets out to discover which nations have had the most successful approach, how the outcomes can be analysed, and attempts to understand what lessons might be learned for future pandemics

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick and Linda are finally back behind the bar at the Vic, and soon receive a visit from DI Pine with the news that Katy has been arrested. But when Stuart listens in, he is desperate to get revenge for his old friend. Stacey gets bad news from her solicitor, prompting Jean into an angry confrontation with Jean and Martin. Kheerat and Suki are unhappy when Vinny gives a statement to the police, and Tiffany accidentally overcharges a customer, giving Dotty a money-making idea

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: Penny-pinching Basil goes behind Sybil's back to save money on structural alterations to the hotel and hires an incompetent cowboy builder to do the job - an underhand tactic that ends up costing him dearly. Classic comedy, starring John Cleese and Prunella Scales

programme.title: Football's Darkest Secret
programme.desc: Three-part documentary exploring historic child abuse that took place in youth football in England from the 1970s to the 1990s and the code of silence surrounding it. The first episode focuses on former footballer Andy Woodward's 2016 revelation of abuse at the hands of a youth coach, and the personal accounts of players from various youth sides

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The grand finale of the drag queen competition has arrived, with choreographer on hand to coach the finalists an through all-singing all-dancing extravaganza to impress judges Michelle Visage, Alan Carr and Graham Norton. All of this year's eliminated queens will be returning to the studio to enjoy the show and see this year's superstar crowned

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team takes some of greatest-ever cars featured in James Bond movies for a spin, including the iconic Aston Martin DB5. Freddie Flintoff gets behind the wheel of an Extreme E race car and puts it to the test against a man in a jet-pack, while Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness test a new Ferrari against a very old Alfa

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood check out some mind-boggling armies from the natural world, including some sleepy suburbs invaded by 10 million bugs

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Two Sherlock fans, sisters Mille and Leila, plan a wedding mystery, featuring bangs, crashes and exploding balloons as their parents try to solve 'the case of the missing rings'

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Two brothers want a den that looks like a Scottish castle

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Viewers do something to Share a Smile all over the UK

programme.title: America's Cup
programme.desc: Action from the latest round of the 36th America's Cup, as Emirates Team New Zealand and Luna Rossa Challenge compete for the famous trophy

programme.title: Richard Hammond's Invisible Worlds
programme.desc: Richard examines some of the hidden wonders that lie beyond the limits of the naked eye. In the first programme, he explores the world of hidden detail during the time it takes the human eye to blink, and uses high-speed cameras to glimpse one of nature's most fleeting spectacles

programme.title: Me and My Dog: The Ultimate Contest
programme.desc: Chris Packham hosts the show where dog owners and their pets compete in physical and mental challenges designed to test their ability to communicate, with trainers and canine scientists on hand to help them develop the relationship between human and animal. In the first episode, half the contestants must lead their dogs through a series of gates using verbal and physical signals, while the others must collect tennis balls in fast-flowing water. The final challenge sees the owners and dogs compete in a tethered biking challenge, to decide who has triumphed in the first stage of the contest

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don journeys to northern Europe, where an abundance of botanical hot spots reveals a culture obsessed with creating gardens. He discovers the floral splendour of Britain's Sissinghurst Castle and the much painted garden of impressionist Monet at Giverny, before witnessing the midnight sun in Norway as he discovers how plants behave 200 miles above the Arctic Circle

programme.title: The Chronicles of Nadiya
programme.desc: Part two of two. After spending a week with her family in Sylhet, Nadiya Hussain cooks her way around Bangladesh and learns more about the country, its people and its food. In the capital city Dhaka, the Great British Bake Off winner spends time with a charity that delivers school meals to deprived children, before learning about an ancient fishing method in a small Hindu village on the banks of the Padma. As her journey continues, she meets and learns from food entrepreneurs and experiences a side of modern Bangladesh she never knew existed

programme.title: The Hairy Bakers
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Simon King travel the UK in search of quality baked British staples - pies, pastries and cakes. They begin by looking at bread and suggesting inexpensive ideas for home baking. Among the recipes they demonstrate are a classic brown loaf, a brown-ale and cheese bread to serve with onion soup, and naan to accompany a curry prepared on the shores of Derbyshire's Derwent reservoir

programme.title: Meet Me in St Louis (1944)
programme.desc: A job offer tempts a banker to consider uprooting his close-knit family and moving to New York. However, his wife and children struggle with the prospect of leaving their friends behind, and are horrified when they realise they might miss out on the 1904 St Louis World's Fair. Meanwhile, teenage daughter Esther faces the abrupt termination of her romance with the boy next door. Musical, starring Judy Garland, Tom Drake and Margaret O'Brien

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin, Caroline Hawley and Mark Stacey head to Dartmoor, where items of interest include a platinum disc awarded for Coldplay's 2005 album X&Y, which is signed by the band's members. Plus, an expert in America talks about a walking stick that is associated with General Tom Thumb

programme.title: Earth's Great Rivers
programme.desc: A journey along the American river, whose fingers stretch into nearly half of the USA, collecting water from 31 states. The documentary follows the Mississippi through varied landscapes from its source to its mouth, starting in the towering Rocky Mountains of Wyoming and Montana, where billions of tons of water, ultimately destined to flow south are trapped as ice and snow

programme.title: DNA Family Secrets
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley helps people answer life-changing questions by examining their DNA. This time, triplets Peter, David and Phillip, who were adopted as young boys, have their DNA analysed by Professor Turi King as they attempt to explore their birth father's heritage. Meanwhile, a woman who was conceived from a sperm donor longs to learn about the other half of her ancestry, and find out if she has any half donor siblings

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: As semi-final week kicks off, the four remaining designers are challenged to transform two restaurants in Hebden Bridge. Judge Michelle Ogundehin reveals that the competitors will be working in pairs, with each designer responsible for a single floor in the restaurant. Who will make it to the final?

programme.title: The 80s with Dominic Sandbrook
programme.desc: The historian continues his personal review of the 1980s, considering how by the middle of the decade, Britain was wrestling with two contradictory impulses - the rise of a strong nationalist sentiment and the emergence of an increasingly globalised world. Along the way Sandbrook examines everything from the invasion of the Falkland Islands to the invasion of the home computer. He examines the moral panic surrounding video nasties, looks at the Americanisation of UK culture and the picket-line skirmishes playing nightly on TV, as well as the Aids epidemic and the threat of the IRA

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: 1985 sees the 'Big Four' of 80's Pop - Spandau, Wham, Culture Club and the Durans - have a relatively quiet year which ushers in a new generation of pop talent. The show becomes a barometer for the state of the nation as the ambitious King and A-ha, propelled by the most expensive video of the year for Take On Me, compete for attention with passionate politicos Billy Bragg and The Style Council who, fired up by the ongoing miners' strike and continuing threat of nuclear war, give the studio audience their all with Between The Wars and Walls Come Tumbling Down. 1985 also sees electro dance hit the mainstream as Dead or Alive top the charts with You Spin Me Round, the first number one for producers Stock, Aitken and Waterman, and Paul Hardcastle holds the top slot for five weeks with 19, a spoken word single made with new sampling technology and released on the 10th anniversary of the end of the Vietnam war. Meanwhile, movie tie-ins and record companies eyeing a 'housewives' market see a

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks a completely random selection of questions this week, with Bill Bailey, Daliso Chaponda, Sally Phillips and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: My Father and Me ()
programme.desc: Documentary maker Nick Broomfield takes a distinctly personal look at his relationship with his humanist-pacifist father Maurice, a factory worker turned photographer of vivid images of postwar England. These images inspired Nick's own film-making career, but also spoke to a difference in outlook between the pair

programme.title: Love & Mercy (2014)
programme.desc: Biopic of the Beach Boys' Brian Wilson, following the singer-songwriter at two periods in his life. In the 1960s, he is at his creative peak, but haunted by inner demons that take their toll on his mental health. By the 1980s, he is a broken man dominated by a shady doctor, until a new relationship offers him a chance at recovery. Starring John Cusack, Paul Dano, Paul Giamatti and Elizabeth Banks

programme.title: The Commune (2016)
programme.desc: A couple establish a commune in a large house in 1970s Copenhagen, encouraging anyone who wants to escape the outside world to join them and their daughter. A succession of unusual characters pass through the collective, but their supposed utopia looks set to be torn apart by an affair within the group. Drama, starring Ulrich Thomsen and Fares Fares. In Danish

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin meets the local Bloom group in Croston, Lancashire, who are determined to win a gold award despite coping with the aftermath of a devastating flood. With only six weeks until judging day, they have come up an ambitious grand plan to impress the judges, but rallying a community that has been through a difficult time is a huge ask

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don welcomes spring at Longmeadow with plenty of changes in the garden to share. As well as getting on with some essential pruning, he lifts and divides perennials, starts sowing chillies and begins growing this year's vegetables. Plus, Carol Klein shares the highlights of the plants that shine out in winter at Bressingham Gardens in Norfolk

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden explores Hampshire by hot-air balloon, beginning at Mottisfont Abbey in Romsey, where she meets a gardening veteran who is preparing to hand over his duties to a young protegee. She visits the Sir Harold Hillier Gardens, and also climbs into a pair of waders for a go at an unusual kind of weeding

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison joins Adam Henson and his team for 48 hours of hectic lambing. She may be the rookie in Adam's lambing shed, but Ellie quickly gets to grips with multiple births, touch-and-go adoptions, and the emotions of the life-or-death decisions faced every day during this busiest of spring seasons on the farm. Tom Heap is on the case to find out why mountains of wool, historically the nation's most valuable sheep product, are now piling up in warehouses across the country

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway goes on another walk around Wales, this time travelling from Porth Swtan to Cemlyn Bay, where he visits a house restored by locals and tries his hand at whittling

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt takes a look back at some of his favourite recipes and best moments from the programme

programme.title: Rick Stein's India
programme.desc: The chef visits Mumbai in search of the bombil, the fish used in Bombay duck, and also enjoys Parsee hospitality and learns about their contribution to Indian cuisine. He then travels south to Pondicherry, where locals prepare Indian dishes with a French twist, before heading north along the coast to Mamallapuram to sample one of the best fish curries he has ever had. Rick also demonstrates how to make popular Parsee chicken dish sali murgh and personal favourite aloo mattar

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Music and worship from around the country

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Nicholas Crane and the team discover the pure pleasure of seaside leisure, seeking out the ideal locations to enjoy their personal passions and experience the joy of the coast

programme.title: Kiss Me Kate (1953)
programme.desc: A divorced husband-and-wife acting duo's constant bickering threatens to disrupts a stage production of Shakespeare's The Taming of the Shrew in which they are the romantic leads. Meanwhile, a case of mistaken identity attracts backstage interlopers in the form of two gangland thugs looking to collect a gambling debt. Adaptation of Cole Porter's Broadway musical comedy, with Howard Keel and Kathryn Grayson

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents as people are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking them to auction

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: New entrant crofting couple Lynn and Sandra send their first pigs to slaughter. Raymond's best bull Major is injured in a brawl. The Rodgers hopes of Balmoral show hang on a clear TB test and buffalo farmer Stevie goes shopping in Holland

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The explorer travels from Texas to southern Mexico, meeting a woman who risks her life to take supplies to migrants stranded on the Mexican side of the border in Reynosa, one of the country's most violent cities, which is dominated by a powerful criminal organisation. He also ventures into the rainforest to explore the Maya city of Yaxchilan, and meets the modern-day descendants of this civilisation, who are increasingly marginalised and treated as second-class citizens

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: The naturalist looks at deception, theft and cheating in the natural world, revealing which species are the leading fraudsters and how they fool others into getting what they want. Chris shows how sea otters kidnap pups and hold them to ransom for food, while white-winged choughs pressgang youngsters from another family into their own so they get extra help to feed their chicks

programme.title: Brazil with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The Monty Python star continues his first visit to Brazil by travelling by river from the northern border with Venezuela to the capital of Brasilia. Along the way he visits indigenous tribe the Yanomami, learning about the threat to their hunter-gatherer way of life, before watching a rehearsal by the Amazon Philharmonic Orchestra and searching for the remains of Henry Ford's unsuccessful attempt to build a vast rubber plantation in the middle of the rainforest. In Belem, music producer Priscilla explains why Amazonian women are such powerful forces, then he moves southwards, meeting rock star and political activist Dinho Ouro Preto, who believes that despite its social and environmental problems, the country is on the brink of becoming a superpower

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian explores the Sahara desert, where temperatures rise to 50 degrees celsius. Guided by Bobo, a local of Berber descent who was born and grew up in the desert, Romesh Ranganathan discovers a place that is vast and inhospitable, impossibly bleak and beautiful

programme.title: The Boy Who Harnessed the Wind (2019)
programme.desc: After a famine forces him to leave school, a 13-year-old boy living with his family in rural Malawi sets his mind to coming up with a solution. First, though, he must persuade his father Trywell to allow him to use parts from his bicycle to build a windmill to irrigate the land. Fact-based drama, directed by and starring Chiwetel Ejiofor, with Maxwell Simba

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Samuel prepares for the arrival of a new addition to his family, turning a deaf ear to Lydia's warnings of the possible consequences. Peter tries to connect with Emma, even though she clearly wants nothing to do with him, and a different side of Sylar emerges as he desperately tries to remember the person he used to be

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Sylar sets out to discover his true identity, assisted and encouraged by Samuel. Meanwhile, Peter tries to locate someone capable of saving Hiro - with a little help from Noah, who manages to track down a boy with healing powers but finds him reluctant to help

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce chairs the topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from a virtual audience around the country

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sahira tells Hanssen that he may not have been Reyhan's only victim, leading the great surgeon to become distracted while operating on her father. Chloe realises Ange is missing and grows increasingly suspicious that Cameron has something to do with her disappearance. Louis gets into an altercation with a former friend who has turned up seeking revenge

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Thomas Forrester and Kate Bliss offer advice as contestants head out into the pouring rain in Shrewsbury to secure items of potential profit under the gavel, where auctioneer Philip Serrell awaits. Love is in the air for presenter Christina Trevanion as she explains how to flirt using a fan

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Married couple Marcus and Paul are paired with chefs Ellis Barrie and Anna Haugh in the fast-and-furious cookery contest. Paul does most of the cooking in their home and he's keen for Marcus to get more involved. The winner will be decided in a hectic 10-Minute Challenge where the chefs must cook against the clock to produce show stopping dishes under pressure. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Kenilworth Castle, near Coventry. Intriguing treasures brought in for valuation include a miniature safe, one of the world's first interactive golf games, and a trilby belonging to an 1980s pop star. Fiona also delves into the story of a car industry entrepreneur who saved the estate from ruin

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Motivational Monday, with Gethin Jones

programme.title: Earth: The Power of the Planet
programme.desc: Dr Iain Stewart investigates the powerful natural forces that shaped the planet over billions of years. Starting with volcanos, he abseils to the edge of an Ethiopian lava lake and explores the underwater caverns believed to have been created by the meteor that wiped out the dinosaurs. The presenter also explains the positive role played by the greenhouse effect many millennia ago in ending the last ice age

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Antiques experts James Braxton and Kate Bliss continue their rivalry at an antiques fair in Peterborough, buying and selling collectibles for the most profit

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Experts James Braxton and Kate Bliss compete over four very different locations to find the best antiques to turn a profit on. It's a tense finale with both investing a lot of money in their final lots as they approach the showdown auction

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives for party food, including Lorraine Pascale's white chocolate lollipops, seafood dishes by James Martin and Rachel Khoo's coq-au-vin on a stick

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores the anatomy and physiology of the largest land animal on the planet, the elephant. Its size seems ill-suited to surviving the most arid regions of Africa, but its inner workings allow it to defy the extreme heat of the desert, and find food and water in seemingly barren landscapes, while its extraordinary memory enables it to repel predators. Here, Chris reveals how hairs on the skin help to keep the elephant cool, and how sensors in its feet may be able to guide it toward rain

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In Morecambe, Lancashire, antiques experts Charles Hanson and Catherine Southon find treasures to take to auction including a vintage Rolex watch. Plus, presenter Paul Martin discovers the rags to riches story of the town's most famous son - Eric Morecambe

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Gary Delaney, Rose Matafeo, Valerie Singleton and Rav Wilding go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in trivia-based games, with a winner declared each Friday

programme.title: Pizza Boys
programme.desc: Following Jez and Ieuan, two young men from Llanelli who had a dream to change their lives and become pizza-makers extraordinaire. The pair decided to buy an old Italian van, put an oven in the back and set off on an adventure to enter the World Pizza Championships in Parma. In the first episode, the pair get some last minute tips from their favourite Cardiff pizzeria, host their own special send-off before packing up their van, Smokey Pete, and hitting the road

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: The food writer and former Great British Bake Off judge continues to celebrate the everyday food and ingredients she has always loved, from hearty and wholesome ideas to easy-to-make crowd-pleasers and family classics. In this edition, Mary focuses on food that exemplifies indulgence, demonstrating mouth-watering dishes that give the mundane a lift with lashings of naughtiness. Treats are in store as she investigates the history of the British pie and reveals the secrets to making an easy sticky toffee pudding

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, asking them questions on the specialist subjects of Stephen Sondheim musicals, dog breeds, Alan Partridge, and the Charles Paris novels by Simon Brett, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell presents as the losing semi-finalists battle it out for third place, with the two teams competing to guess the connections between things that seem utterly random at first glance. One of the sequence asks what links Heisenbug, Boggart, Weeping angel, and Schroedinger's cat

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The first of the semi-finals sees two teams of four students battling it out to make it to the climax of the competition and the chance to take home the trophy. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti visit a hi-tech hotel in the self-governing Chinese gambling capital of Macau. Monica gets special access to a secret set of VIP suites dubbed The Mansion, where it takes more than just money to book an exclusive room. Giles visits a special theatre that sends its audience flying through the air, and helps to arrange the flowers for the Chinese New Year celebrations

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Black Mirror creator Charlie Brooker hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz from December 2019, with broadcaster Emma Barnett and comedian Phil Wang joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: A Week in the Wild
programme.desc: Ex-politician Stanley Johnson, broadcaster and model Melanie Sykes, and comedian Henning Wehn tackle an iconic long-distance hike in Mexico's Copper Canyon, where they learn about the country's culture, people and environment

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison joins Adam Henson and his team for 48 hours of hectic lambing. She may be the rookie in Adam's lambing shed, but Ellie quickly gets to grips with multiple births, touch-and-go adoptions, and the emotions of the life-or-death decisions faced every day during this busiest of spring seasons on the farm. Tom Heap is on the case to find out why mountains of wool, historically the nation's most valuable sheep product, are now piling up in warehouses across the country

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste pick up the case of a man who lost thousands of pounds and his home after falling in love with a woman claiming to be a gold trader. The story takes a bizarre twist as the mysterious woman known as Maryanne starts making financial demands of them too

programme.title: My Unique B&B
programme.desc: Master craftsman Simon Parfett and his team are in Bradford-on-Avon to help single mother Fiona transform her 110-year-old narrowboat into a thriving short-rental business. Faced with an almost empty shell, it looks like being a big job. Before long, Zoe is painting and renovating the interior, while Simon and Ian maximise the space with foldout beds and a copper-fronted bath in the stern

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Alaska: Earth's Frozen Kingdom
programme.desc: The land is bathed in 24-hour sunlight during the short Alaskan summer, luring hummingbirds up from Mexico, exposing deserts in the Arctic and relying on parachuting firefighters to tackle forest fires in its vast wilderness. Grizzly bears fatten up on a sudden wealth of salmon, a caribou calf joins a vast herd on its great migration and a huge male moose finds unlikely ways to impress a female. Among the human population, a dedicated grower of giant vegetables takes his half-ton pumpkin to the Alaska State Fair

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Paul Rose tells the story of Captain James Cook's historic 1768 voyage to find a vast southern continent. No such land mass was discovered, but the crew of the Endeavour went on to Tahiti and Australia, which Cook claimed for Britain. The ship was almost ripped apart on the Great Barrier Reef, but the captain's navigational skills eventually steered it back home to safety

programme.title: Land of Mine (2015)
programme.desc: In post-Second World War Denmark, a group of young German POWs are forced to clear a beach of thousands of landmines under the watch of a Danish sergeant who slowly learns to appreciate their plight. Drama, starring Roland Moller, Louis Hofmann and Joel Basman. In German, Danish and English

programme.title: Double Agent: The Eddie Chapman Story
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre revisits the story of wartime double agent Eddie Chapman, the subject matter of his best-selling book Agent Zigzag. He reveals how, during the Second World War, Chapman was able to dupe Nazi Germany so successfully that he was awarded the Iron Cross, the country's most prestigious decoration. Includes footage from an interview the former spy gave three years before his death in 1997

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Mark Goodier hosts the edition first aired on October 18, with performances by artists including A-ha, Aztec Camera and Mick Jones, Innocence, Whitney Houston, Happy Mondays, Rita MacNeil, Hi Tek 3 featuring Ya Kid K, Maria McKee and Belinda Carlisle

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles hosts the edition first aired on October 25, with performances by artists including Belinda Carlisle, Public Image Ltd, the Beautiful South, Berlin, Paul McCartney, the Righteous Brothers, the Soup Dragons, Paul Simon, Roxette and Jason Donovan

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Midge Ure, lead singer of Ultravox and one of the men behind Band Aid, and Kim Appleby, who had a string of hits with her sister Mel in the Stock, Aitken & Waterman-produced band Mel and Kim, revisit the 1980s to determine why certain cities produced their own diverse tunes. In the first episode, they explore London's New Romantic movement, travel to Coventry to investigate the rise of Ska and speak to some of Sheffield's electronic music pioneers

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Paul Rose tells the story of Captain James Cook's historic 1768 voyage to find a vast southern continent. No such land mass was discovered, but the crew of the Endeavour went on to Tahiti and Australia, which Cook claimed for Britain. The ship was almost ripped apart on the Great Barrier Reef, but the captain's navigational skills eventually steered it back home to safety

programme.title: Alaska: Earth's Frozen Kingdom
programme.desc: The land is bathed in 24-hour sunlight during the short Alaskan summer, luring hummingbirds up from Mexico, exposing deserts in the Arctic and relying on parachuting firefighters to tackle forest fires in its vast wilderness. Grizzly bears fatten up on a sudden wealth of salmon, a caribou calf joins a vast herd on its great migration and a huge male moose finds unlikely ways to impress a female. Among the human population, a dedicated grower of giant vegetables takes his half-ton pumpkin to the Alaska State Fair

programme.title: The Greatest Poem of World War One: David Jones's In Parenthesis
programme.desc: Poet and author Owen Sheers traces the inspirations behind David Jones's In Parenthesis, the celebrated First World War poem that received acclaim from the likes of TS Eliot and WH Auden. Unlike many of his contemporaries, Jones only published his magnus opus in 1937, some 19 years after the end of the Great War, but the poem reflected the the entirety of his wartime experiences. Having remains a private throughout the whole conflict, Jones was uniquely equipped to detail the realities of life on the front-lines, and he fought for longer than any other British writer. Here, Sheers reads key passages, and tracks the construction of the epic poem from pomp and order of the English parade ground to the carnage of the Somme, and the horrors of Jones's campaign in Mametz Wood in northern France. Includes contributions by Simon Armitage

programme.title: The NHS: To Provide All People
programme.desc: A film poem to mark the birth of the NHS, charting a day in the life of a present day regional hospital. Written by Owen Sheers and performed by a stellar cast of acting talent, including Michael Sheen, Eve Myles, Martin Freeman, Sian Phillips, Jonathan Pryce, Celia Imrie, Meera Syal, Susan Wokoma, Michelle Fairley, Rhashan Stone, Michelle Collins and Tamsin Greig

programme.title: Railway Nation: A Journey in Verse
programme.desc: Sabrina Mahfouz, Michael Symmons Roberts, Liz Berry, Andrew McMillan, Imtiaz Dharker and Sean O'Brien celebrate Britain's love of trains by reinventing WH Auden and Benjamin Britten's train journey across Britain. The pair made their trip almost 80 years ago, travelling from London to Edinburgh on board an overnight mail train, as part of the production of the ground-breaking 1936 documentary Night Mail. Now, the six poets make a similar trip - this time during the day - to document the voices of rail travellers from all walks of life going about their daily business

programme.title: Sylvia (2003)
programme.desc: Biopic of troubled poet and novelist Sylvia Plath whose creativity was deeply affected by her long and ultimately futile battle with depression. The film explores the impact of her mental state on her turbulent marriage to Ted Hughes, culminating in her eventual downward spiral into despair. Starring Gwyneth Paltrow, Daniel Craig, Jared Harris and Amira Casar

programme.title: Performance Live
programme.desc: The first in an occasional strand broadcast over the course of the next two years that showcase innovative live theatre, dance and comedy performances devised specially for TV. Spoken-word artist Kate Tempest kicks proceedings off with a night of poetry at the Rivoli Ballroom in south London, where she performs her new tale Let Them Eat Chaos, as well as introducing appearances by Deanna Rodger, David J Pugilist and Isaiah Hull, who offer their own reflections on life in contemporary Britain

programme.title: The NHS: To Provide All People
programme.desc: A film poem to mark the birth of the NHS, charting a day in the life of a present day regional hospital. programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar seeks inspiration as she explores Wensleydale, setting out from Leyburn on a walk to Bolton Castle, where Mary, Queen of Scots was imprisoned in 1568. Along the way, Shanaz discovers evidence of an industrial past and talks to a volunteer worker on the heritage railway line, while also stopping to sketch and reflect

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paints a wintry mountain scene using only grey and white tones

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and art expert Philip Mould investigate the first work of sculpture featured on the show, an unusual piece called The Gazing Head, which may have been made by Alberto Giacometti in 1930s Paris. The quest to uncover the truth is complicated by the fact that the sculpture was once broken into several pieces by a cat

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: The story of a widowed 83-year-old who went undercover in a Chilean nursing home to investigate a private detective's concerns that the residents, including her father, were being abused. Despite an initial struggle with the technology involved, the aged spy adapted to his role, only to discover that the truth about the institution was very different to what his client had expected

programme.title: Morocco to Timbuktu: An Arabian Adventure
programme.desc: Writer and explorer Alice Morrison presents the first of two programmes in which she follows the salt roads of North Africa, once the world's richest trading networks, to the fabled sandstone city of Timbuktu. Setting off from Tangier, she learns how the routes were forged by the demand for gold from the mines of sub-Saharan Africa. She then passes through the Islamic city Fes, home to the world's oldest university, catches a train to the great market town of Marrakech and treks across the Atlas Mountains, before visiting the ancient caves of salt, the commodity that gave the roads their name

programme.title: Morocco to Timbuktu: An Arabian Adventure
programme.desc: Writer and explorer Alice Morrison embarks on a camel trek over the dunes of the Sahara, 3.5 million square miles of desert, for the second and final part of her journey to Timbuktu. Along the way she visits a livestock market that centuries ago provided merchants with their camels, before reaching her final destination. Far from the legendary City of Gold, Timbuktu is a city where UN solders keep a fragile peace after an incursion by Islamic extremists in 2012. Alice relives the city's glorious past with a visit to its world famous mosques, meets the Tuaregs - desert nomads who have sought refuge in there - and celebrates at a party with her newfound friends

She also adds some canned tuna, green olives, and boiled egg. She seasons the mixture with paprika, black pepper and her special ingredient, oregano. Then Lola spreads the filling over the dough, which has been in the oven. She finishes the recipe by adding the last piece of dough on top and then putting it back in the oven to cook. programme.title: Garden Tales
programme.desc: Thelma and Louise, the garden’s two Berkshire pigs, love nothing more than taking it easy. But today, they’re in for a shock. Their nephews and nieces are coming to visit and there are loads of them. Thelma and Louise have no idea what to do with this boisterous bunch, but fortunately some of the other garden’s residents are on hand to help out - or not!

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects associated with the colour blue, from the sea to the sky. We also explore the bigger picture of our blue planet. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour blue.

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: Animation following the adventure of a group of animals living on a patchwork blanket

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join seven-month-old Rosie and Fionn as they discover the world around them and see how their eyesight is improving. Fionn and his brother Odhrán are playing a game of peek-a-boo with mum and dad at home. Then the whole family head over to Loch Ness, where there are lots of exciting things to see. They even use some binoculars! Over in Ireland, Rosie’s brother Donnacha picks some colourful flowers at the garden centre to take home and show him. Back at home, Fionn is seeing more than his lunch, but what else can he see? In Rosie’s garden they plant their flowers and are inspired to finger-paint using all the colours from the rainbow, while Fionn’s family does a treasure hunt in the garden. They have to find their presents fast as someone on four legs is also hunting! Rosie’s family finish the day with a great game of hide-and-seek, searching high and low until everyone is found.

programme.title: What's On Your Head?

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Febo's family are from America, where they celebrate Thanksgiving. As it is traditional on Thanksgiving to prepare and serve pumpkin pie as a dessert, Febo is making this for his friends. He starts by making a pastry case and fitting it into a pie dish. For the pumpkin filling: he pours melted butter and buttermilk into a bowl of brown sugar, along with whisked egg, cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg, allspice and cloves. Febo adds his special ingredient, pumpkin puree, and then mixes them all together. When it's mixed, he spoons it into the empty pastry case and puts it in the oven to cook. Febo's friends all help to decorate his American-themed table before they taste his pumpkin pie.

Rory and his son Sandy search for snails in the garden and put on a very exciting snail race, while Ferne meets some very rare snails and holds a Giant African Land Snail at the zoo. Plus, we find out why snails are so important to the environment. They play an important part in a giant food web.

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. It’s Safari Worlds annual recycling event, and Andy has been busy tidying up and encouraging the guests to recycle their litter. Mr Hammond calls as he is worried that the new clownfish are not settling in okay after their long trip from Borneo. They are leaving the safety of their sea anemone home, which they hardly ever do in the wild. Mr Hammond is at a loss as to why they are doing this, but Andy reassures him that they will get to the bottom of it. It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! Uploading his wet suit, aqua pack and gizmo, Andy runs to the Aqua Dome’s loading dock and boards his safari-sub, a hi-tech underwater submersible capable of travelling to any location on the planet in the blink of an eye. With analysis from John Barclay and Jeremy Guscott and commentary by Andrew Cotter, Brian Moore and Andy Nicol

The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a new family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling, with a variety of entertaining rounds and a cash prize at stake

programme.title: The Wall

programme.desc: France v Wales (Kick-off 8.00pm). All the action from the match in the fifth round of fixtures, held at Stade de France. Les Bleus clinched a 27-23 victory when the sides met at Principality Stadium last year, but Wales prevailed 24-19 on their last visit here in 2019, and should head into this match in a confident mood after easily beating rivals England 40-24 in the third round of fixtures. With analysis from Sam Warburton, Martin Johnson and Thomas Castaignede, and commentary by Eddie Butler, Jonathan Davies and Benjamin Kayser

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents action from today's sixth-round ties - Bournemouth v Southampton at Vitality Stadium and Everton v Manchester City at Goodison Park. programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Brighton & Hove Albion v Newcastle United. Gary Lineker presents highlights of the Premier League match at Amex Stadium. Plus, a look back at Friday's clash between Fulham and Leeds United at Craven Cottage

programme.desc: Stars of stage, screen and the movies gather to entertain the nation with hilarious sketches, live performances, big surprises and stunning music acts in a celebration to raise much-needed cash for good causes at home and across the world. Hosted by Lenny Henry, Davina McCall, Paddy McGuinness, Alesha Dixon and David Tennant

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Sheffield United (Kick-off 1.30pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the sixth-round tie at Stamford Bridge. The Blues are looking to reach the semi-finals for the fourth time in five seasons and enter this match as strong favourites given the struggles of the Blades in this season's Premier League. The sides have also met twice in the league this season, with Chelsea prevailing 4-1 here in November and 2-1 at Bramall Lane last month. With analysis from Micah Richards

programme.title: Pizza Boys
programme.desc: Jez and Ieuz have one time for one last masterclass in Napoli before leaving for Parma. There they pick up a final sprinkling of parmesan, mix the most important batch of dough they will ever make and try to get a good night's sleep before the competition. Then destiny awaits as they face the best pizzaiolos on the planet at the Pizza World Championship

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Leicester City v Manchester United (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the sixth-round tie at King Power Stadium, as the line-up for the semi-finals is confirmed. United are unbeaten in their last 13 league and cup matches against Leicester, but the Foxes are enjoying a strong season so far and the sides shared the spoils in a 2-2 draw when they met here in the Premier League on Boxing Day. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Dion Dublin

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison celebrates the arrival of Spring on her home turf in the Cotswolds. From the millions of snowdrops in Painswick's Rococo Gardens to discovering some old folklore, finding out about a scheme to enable wheelchair users to enjoy tougher terrain in the great outdoors and meeting the cattle that encourage wildflowers, Ellie also raids the Countryfile Archive, revisiting the time she went on dolphin watch in Cardigan Bay, Matt Baker visited tulip fields in Lincolnshire, and Adam Henson went to North Yorkshire to catch his supper

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team takes some of greatest-ever cars featured in James Bond movies for a spin, including the iconic Aston Martin DB5. programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Jed Mercurio's smash-hit crime thriller returns for its sixth instalment, with Martin Compston, Vicky McClure and Adrian Dunbar reprising their roles as DS Steve Arnott, DI Kate Fleming and Superintendent Ted Hastings. New information comes to light about a possible suspect in a year-long unsolved murder, and DCI Joanne Davidson (Kelly Macdonald) appears keen to finally bring Gail Vella's killer to justice. However, her conduct soon arouses suspicion, and it's not long before AC-12 are on the case. But as their investigation uncovers deeper and darker information, will Davidson turn out to be their most dangerous adversary yet?
