programme.desc: The Welsh consumer show fighting for your rights. With Lucy Owen, Rachel Treadaway Williams, Steffan Powell and Mo Jannah

programme.title: Football's Darkest Secret
programme.desc: Three-part documentary exploring historic child abuse that took place in youth football in England from the 1970s to the 1990s and the code of silence surrounding it. The first episode focuses on former footballer Andy Woodward's 2016 revelation of abuse at the hands of a youth coach, and the personal accounts of players from various youth sides

programme.title: Fawlty Towers
programme.desc: Penny-pinching Basil goes behind Sybil's back to save money on structural alterations to the hotel and hires an incompetent cowboy builder to do the job - an underhand tactic that ends up costing him dearly. Classic comedy, starring John Cleese and Prunella Scales

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The grand finale of the drag queen competition has arrived, with choreographer on hand to coach the finalists an through all-singing all-dancing extravaganza to impress judges Michelle Visage, Alan Carr and Graham Norton. All of this year's eliminated queens will be returning to the studio to enjoy the show and see this year's superstar crowned

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team takes some of greatest-ever cars featured in James Bond movies for a spin, including the iconic Aston Martin DB5. Freddie Flintoff gets behind the wheel of an Extreme E race car and puts it to the test against a man in a jet-pack, while Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness test a new Ferrari against a very old Alfa

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by guests Sabrina Ghayour, Tom Booton and Richard Curtis. Olly Smith joins by video link, and selects wines to accompany the meals prepared in the studio

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts this celebrity edition of the kitchen duel, which sees actor Stephen Mangan squaring up against barrister and TV judge Robert Rinder in a battle of the pots and pans. Paired with chefs Jeremy Pang and Ellis Barrie, they must create dishes from their £5.00 bag of ingredients in just 20 minutes - and with everything from a spicy noodle soup to a massive mushroom vol-au-vent on offer, it promises to be a close call

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by Karen Carney and Dion Dublin to look ahead to the sixth round of the FA Cup

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp challenges two teams to search for bargains at an antiques fair in the grounds of Kedleston Hall, Derbyshire. John Cameron and Tim Weeks offer expert advice

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Scotland v Italy (Kick-off 2.15pm). All the action from the match in the fifth round of fixtures, held at BT Murrayfield. Scotland have won the last nine meetings between the sides and will expect to extend that run today given their superior form in this year's championship. With analysis from John Barclay and Jeremy Guscott and commentary by Andrew Cotter, Brian Moore and Andy Nicol

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie is joined by Steven Thompson to present the classified results and a round-up of the afternoon's main football news. Plus, a look back at the week's talking points

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites Amanda Abbington, Rick Edwards, Richard Blackwood and Laura Tobin to take to the black chair. In the first round, they are put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects - Phoebe from Friends, An American Werewolf in London, Bon Jovi, and the octopus. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a new family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling, with a variety of entertaining rounds and a cash prize at stake

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Danny Dyer hosts the game show, which sees an engaged couple of teachers from Essex answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck. They hope to put the prize money towards a new home for themselves and their baby daughter

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v Wales (Kick-off 8.00pm). All the action from the match in the fifth round of fixtures, held at Stade de France. Les Bleus clinched a 27-23 victory when the sides met at Principality Stadium last year, but Wales prevailed 24-19 on their last visit here in 2019, and should head into this match in a confident mood after easily beating rivals England 40-24 in the third round of fixtures. With analysis from Sam Warburton, Martin Johnson and Thomas Castaignede, and commentary by Eddie Butler, Jonathan Davies and Benjamin Kayser

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents action from today's sixth-round ties - Bournemouth v Southampton at Vitality Stadium and Everton v Manchester City at Goodison Park. Bournemouth were the only Championship side remaining in the competition but will have been hoping to make home advantage count against their South Coast rivals, while the Citizens were looking to emulate their 3-1 away win over Everton in the league last month

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Brighton & Hove Albion v Newcastle United. Gary Lineker presents highlights of the Premier League match at Amex Stadium. Both teams were in need of points to boost their chances of avoiding relegation, and it was Brighton who were comfortable 3-0 victors when the sides met at St James' Park in September. Plus, a look back at Friday's clash between Fulham and Leeds United at Craven Cottage

programme.desc: Jonathan Sutherland presents action from the latest Premiership fixtures, including Dundee United v Aberdeen and Livingston v Hibernian

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show

programme.title: The Sunday Show
programme.desc: Martin Geissler presents the main issues from the world of politics and beyond, featuring interviews, discussion and analysis on the stories that matter to Scotland

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents moral and religious discussions on topical issues, with contributions by members of the studio audience

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay
programme.desc: Religious thoughts from Scotland, plus a selection of hymns

programme.title: Comic Relief 2021
programme.desc: Stars of stage, screen and the movies gather to entertain the nation with hilarious sketches, live performances, big surprises and stunning music acts in a celebration to raise much-needed cash for good causes at home and across the world. Hosted by Lenny Henry, Davina McCall, Paddy McGuinness, Alesha Dixon and David Tennant

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Sheffield United (Kick-off 1.30pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the sixth-round tie at Stamford Bridge. The Blues are looking to reach the semi-finals for the fourth time in five seasons and enter this match as strong favourites given the struggles of the Blades in this season's Premier League. The sides have also met twice in the league this season, with Chelsea prevailing 4-1 here in November and 2-1 at Bramall Lane last month. With analysis from Micah Richards

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers compete to design a garden for a family of seven in Stamford, Lincolnshire, who want a peaceful garden that they can call their own

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Leicester City v Manchester United (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the sixth-round tie at King Power Stadium, as the line-up for the semi-finals is confirmed. United are unbeaten in their last 13 league and cup matches against Leicester, but the Foxes are enjoying a strong season so far and the sides shared the spoils in a 2-2 draw when they met here in the Premier League on Boxing Day. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Dion Dublin

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison celebrates the arrival of Spring on her home turf in the Cotswolds. From the millions of snowdrops in Painswick's Rococo Gardens to discovering some old folklore, finding out about a scheme to enable wheelchair users to enjoy tougher terrain in the great outdoors and meeting the cattle that encourage wildflowers, Ellie also raids the Countryfile Archive, revisiting the time she went on dolphin watch in Cardigan Bay, Matt Baker visited tulip fields in Lincolnshire, and Adam Henson went to North Yorkshire to catch his supper

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team takes some of greatest-ever cars featured in James Bond movies for a spin, including the iconic Aston Martin DB5. Freddie Flintoff gets behind the wheel of an Extreme E race car and puts it to the test against a man in a jet-pack, while Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness test a new Ferrari against a very old Alfa

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Jed Mercurio's smash-hit crime thriller returns for its sixth instalment, with Martin Compston, Vicky McClure and Adrian Dunbar reprising their roles as DS Steve Arnott, DI Kate Fleming and Superintendent Ted Hastings. New information comes to light about a possible suspect in a year-long unsolved murder, and DCI Joanne Davidson (Kelly Macdonald) appears keen to finally bring Gail Vella's killer to justice. However, her conduct soon arouses suspicion, and it's not long before AC-12 are on the case. But as their investigation uncovers deeper and darker information, will Davidson turn out to be their most dangerous adversary yet?

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of today's Premier League fixtures - West Ham United v Arsenal at London Stadium and Aston Villa v Tottenham Hotspur at Villa Park, as all the teams involved looked to boost their hopes of playing European football next season

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Action from the concluding sixth-round ties - Chelsea v Sheffield United at Bramall Lane and Leicester City v Manchester United at King Power Stadium

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Celtic v Rangers. Steven Thompson presents action from the Old Firm derby at Celtic Park, as the hosts looked for a morale-boosting victory over the recently crowned champions

programme.title: Bill Bailey: Limboland
programme.desc: The comedian muses on people's expectations of happiness in a performance recorded at the Hammersmith Apollo. He shares the story of a disastrous family trip to see the Northern Lights, recalls a meeting with Paul McCartney that did not go as expected and performs a heart-rending country and western ballad on the nature of love

programme.desc: Dan Walker and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick and Linda are finally back behind the bar at the Vic, and soon receive a visit from DI Pine with the news that Katy has been arrested. programme.title: Scran With My Nan
programme.desc: Two young people try to resolve their problems and make big decisions about their personal lives, while making and eating dinner with their grandmothers. Former Love Island contestant Theo is torn between starting his own business and maintaining his celebrity profile, while social media star Rae needs help with her love life. Meals prepared across the generation gap include a steak supper, Jamaican jerk chicken with rice and peas, pulled pork tacos and a chicken pie

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The grand finale of the drag queen competition has arrived, with choreographer on hand to coach the finalists an through all-singing all-dancing extravaganza to impress judges Michelle Visage, Alan Carr and Graham Norton. All of this year's eliminated queens will be returning to the studio to enjoy the show and see this year's superstar crowned

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team takes some of greatest-ever cars featured in James Bond movies for a spin, including the iconic Aston Martin DB5. Freddie Flintoff gets behind the wheel of an Extreme E race car and puts it to the test against a man in a jet-pack, while Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness test a new Ferrari against a very old Alfa

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: As the government faces mounting criticism that well-connected people made millions out of Britain's PPE crisis, Panorama investigates who won out. More than £12billion was spent in the first six months of the pandemic on contracts to provide personal protective equipment. Reporter Richard Bilton meets one man who made £40million on a deal, and speaks to others who felt ignored in favour of less experienced suppliers. As the government refuses to reveal the full details of all its so-called VIP deals, the programme reveals the high-profile connections to one lucrative contract

programme.title: Farpaisean Chon-Chaorach (Sheepdog Trials)
programme.desc: Donald MacSween and Catriona MacPhee present the best of the action from the second day of the Scottish National Sheep Dog Trial in Brisbane Glen Estate, West Kilbride. The weather holds, but it's a difficult course for the handlers. A variety of sheep means it's the luck of the draw what packet the handlers get, with some being more stubborn than others. Good runs from Jimmy Menzies and Ross Henderson put them in a good position to make the team

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean tries to redecorate the hall, but the landlady's nasty one-eyed cat obstructs him at every turn - until he puts it in the dustbin

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Faced with a long shopping list from his formidable landlady, the hapless half-wit builds a giant motorised trolley from a petrol mower and pram wheels, hoping to get the job done in double-quick time

programme.title: Scrambled!

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike is delighted when Iris invites him over for a cup of tea on the terrace, but with the raccoons to contend with, will he make it to his date on time

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows a young boy and his two friends as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Double bill. Robin gets amnesia and starts thinking that Hawkman is his mother. Then, it's the Teen Titans vs Titans East as the two teams compete for who gets Bumblebee

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda: The Paws of Destiny
programme.desc: The gang learns of even bigger and even worse forces working against the Empress and the Kingdom of China and must come up with a plan to put a stop to them before it is too late.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Dave is warned by local fortune teller Madam Donald that walking under a ladder will bring bad luck - he walks under one anyway, and soon a meteor is heading for Grimsby. It is up to Dave and his friend Robert Robot to catch it before everyone is squished.

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James serves up a feast of food for This Morning's resident medic Ranj Singh, including recipes for risotto, lemon sole, salmon en croute and rhubarb crumble cake. Plus, chefs Sat Bains and Bettina Campolucci Bordi drop by with delicious dishes of their own

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef shares his love of new produce from the gardens and explains why seasonality is important and why everyone should embrace it. From his Oxfordshire kitchen, he cooks pan-fried salmon with spinach and chive butter sauce, and rustles up a fricassee of wild mushrooms. Long-time friend Jean-Christophe Novelli pays a visit and bakes him an indulgent tarte tatin, and baked apple with caramel

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef visits the Languedoc region, famous for its wine and the hearty, meaty dish cassoulet, and stops at a roadside fruit stall owned by a couple of British expats, before creating an unusual dish using melon and onion rings. He also wanders round a museum dedicated to Citroen 2CVs and cooks a steak on a barbecue in the blazing sunshine

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Bullard asks Barnaby to investigate the disappearance of a friend's daughter who was a member of the Oblong Foundation, a cult based at Malham Hall. Jones goes under cover as a new recruit to solve the mystery, but he and Barnaby soon start digging into an old case involving the death of a couple in a boat explosion, which could be linked to the missing woman. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes, with Will Knightley, Kevin Doyle, Holly Aird, Barbara Flynn and John Woodvine

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: Ireland v England (Kick-off 4.45pm). Coverage of both teams' concluding match of the championship, held at Aviva Stadium. England prevailed 24-12 when the sides met at Twickenham last year and also won 32-20 here in 2019, but they have made an unconvincing defence of their title and Ireland will fancy their chances on this occasion despite also enduring a far from flawless campaign. Mark Pougatch presents, with analysis from Jonny Wilkinson, Clive Woodward, Brian O'Driscoll and Rory Best, and commentary by Nick Mullins, Lawrence Dallaglio and Geordan Murphy

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Saturday Night Takeaway
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan is in the guest announcer booth, while Cat Deeley is in the studio for one of the riskiest Undercovers ever as Ant and Dec wreak havoc on their former SMTV Live co-star. After 20 years, the trio also reunite with a whole host of celebrity pals for the return of their much-loved sitcom Chums. Plus, Stephen Mulhern is back for another sporting challenge and a member of the virtual audience gets the chance to Win the Ads

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis presents the climax of the spinning-chair singing contest as the last four acts battle it out for the chance to win a recording contract, with coaches Tom Jones, Anne-Marie, Olly Murs and Will.i.am looking on

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show: Special Guests
programme.desc: Another selection of the most memorable moments from recent series of the talk show. Among the guests featured are David Attenborough, Sheridan Smith, Kevin Hart, Michael Caine, Julie Walters, Hugh Jackman, Liam Gallagher, Michelle Keegan, Ed Sheeran, Tiffany Haddish and Robbie Williams.

programme.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day (1991)
programme.desc: A reprogrammed killing machine is sent back in time to the 20th century to protect John Connor, a teenage boy whose destiny is to save the human race. After breaking John's mother out of a mental institution, the pair set out to avert a future nuclear war - pursued by an advanced prototype terminator with shape-changing powers. Sci-fi action adventure sequel, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Linda Hamilton, Edward Furlong and Robert Patrick

programme.desc: Magoo thinks that Fizz is a hat he is going to wear for his romantic date with Marnie. That's bad enough, but Fizz has a very important job to do!

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: At the supermarket, Magoo mixes his shopping cart up with a baby stroller. Fizz decides to save the toddler and become a media star.

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Lady Penelope and Parker are testing out FAB2, a luxury yacht, when they receive a distress call from a nearby tanker. A Tsunami has erupted, and a massive wave is headed for the stalled tanker's location

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The police suspect Bean of stealing his landlady's jewellery - but the real culprit is a magpie he's been nursing back to health

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: While Mike is distracted by flirting with Iris, the raccoons sneak into the house

programme.title: DC Superhero Girls
programme.desc: Diana befriends a new student who has moved to Metropolis from Japan, named Tatsu. But is she everything she seems to be?

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: An immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Anna is training to be a magician with Dave as her helpful assistant, but their disappearing trick is not up to much - that is, until Zombie Katie bestows a magic spell book upon them. Soon they can't stop making stuff disappear, but what will happen when there is nothing left to make disappear in Grimsby?

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: At 694 metres in height, Pen-y-ghent is the smallest fell on the Yorkshire Three Peaks Challenge, but the dramatic summit affords far-reaching views above the Dales, with plenty of surprises en route. The peak lies 3km east of Horton in Ribblesdale and the Pennine Way links the summit to the village. As Julia reveals, the route is around 5km in length, as the Way curves initially to the north before turning east to reach the summit

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan welcomes former boxer Frank Bruno and actor Stephen Mangan, while David Domoney has the perfect fruit trees for any patch, and there is a selection of the best British fruity tipples

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef shares his love of new produce from the gardens and explains why seasonality is important and why everyone should embrace it. From his Oxfordshire kitchen, he cooks pan-fried salmon with spinach and chive butter sauce, and rustles up a fricassee of wild mushrooms. Long-time friend Jean-Christophe Novelli pays a visit and bakes him an indulgent tarte tatin, and baked apple with caramel

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team create a garden for a family with twin girls who have a serious, life-limiting condition. Three-year-olds Darcie and Evie Chapman have been diagnosed with Dravet syndrome, a very rare form of epilepsy, and parents Mark and Natalie must be on permanent watch to prevent their daughters' seizures leading to something more serious. The Chapmans' current outdoor space is unsuitable for the girls, with changes of levels, colours of plants and even the light through branches influencing their condition, so the crew follows the rules of a formal garden where straight lines, order and symmetry give an unfussy overall look

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec present a selection of memorable, surprising and extraordinary auditions from the various editions of the talent show franchise from around the world, including those of X, Callum Scott and Kseniya Simonova

programme.title: Minions (2015)
programme.desc: A race of strange, babbling yellow creatures has served the cause of evil throughout the history of the world. In the 1960s, three of these eternal henchmen get a job working for a criminal genius who is hatching a plot against the British crown - but while carrying out her orders, they uncover a threat to their whole species. Animated comedy, with the voices of Sandra Bullock, Pierre Coffin, Jon Hamm and Michael Keaton

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Compilation of some of the most dramatic endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Susan Calman, Dermot Murnaghan, Perri Shakes-Drayton and Omid Djalili pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: All Star Musicals
programme.desc: John Barrowman hosts a show as celebrities Ranj Singh, Robert Peston, Rob Rinder, Barney Walsh, Jessica Hynes and Luisa Bradshaw-White perform songs from hit musicals such as Chicago, The Greatest Showman, Guys and Dolls and Beauty & the Beast. They will be trying to impress musical theatre stars Elaine Paige, Samantha Barks and Trevor Dion Nicholas, as well as the viewing audience

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local aristocrats and beekeepers the Deddingtons have built up a thriving empire, producing a honey with extraordinary healing power that has put the village of Granville Norton on the map. But how far will they go to ensure they are never dethroned? Griff Rhys Jones and Imogen Stubbs guest star in the mystery drama, with Neil Dudgeon and Nick Hendrix

programme.title: DNA Journey
programme.desc: Britain's Got Talent judge Amanda Holden and comedian Alan Carr embark on journeys across the country as they delve into their family histories, unlocking secrets of the past and meeting close family they never knew existed. Amanda's journey starts in the grounds of a stately home in Cornwall, while Alan's hunt for his ancestors takes him to a pub in Erith with something of a colourful past

programme.title: Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Ugo Monye presents action from the fifth round of fixtures, featuring France v Wales at Stade de France, Ireland v England at Aviva Stadium and Scotland v Italy at BT Murrayfield. With analysis from Maggie Alphonsi and Gareth Thomas

programme.title: 2020: The Story of Us
programme.desc: Oscar-winning director Kevin Macdonald's feature-length documentary tells the story of coronavirus in Britain through the experience of people immersed in its impact, both personally and professionally. Using testimony from staff in hospitals - consultants, doctors and nurses - as well as weaving in the experience of patients, this documentary paints a vivid portrait of the working lives of ICU staff and those they treat during a crisis which proves both challenging, life-changing and often tragic for those caught in it

programme.title: Tenable

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Olivia, Albie, Tamrin and Neal pit their wits against a ruthless quiz genius in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paul is panicked, Liv is suspicious, and Will does his best to distract Kim

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tyrone and Alina try to convince Seb there's nothing going on. Sitting in Nick's car, Leanne is counting her ill gotten gains when a police officer knocks on the window. Amy and Aadi are quick to assume that Brian is their online troll

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team are in Dorset creating an exciting garden for a young family. His idea for the Drakes is to give them an outdoor space that reflects terminally ill dad Andrew's love of the great outdoors and his passion for working with wood. He opts for a garden with a frontier-land theme that Andrew, wife Rachel and their young son Lucas can enjoy, creating a prairie-inspired back yard of big scale rustic wood structures, agricultural motifs with craftsmanship and the spirit of adventure at its core

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: While Fiz is humiliated, Tyrone admits he has fallen for Alina. Elsewhere, Leanne stashes her burner phone before telling Harvey she wants out, and Daisy offers to help Sean out, at a price

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Cassie and Sunny interview two of the suspects again and get closer to the truth of what happened to Walsh. Balcombe believes she may have found the cause of death. Unexpectedly, things take a devastating turn for Cassie

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Coleen Nolan chats to Piers Morgan about her life in the spotlight, from her first stage appearance, in Blackpool, at the age of two, to selling more than 25 million records with the Nolans. The Loose Women star also opens up about the cancer 'curse' that has devastated her family, and reveals that she is considering a mastectomy after seeing three of her sisters battle the disease, including Bernie, who passed away in 2013

programme.title: Bear Grylls Wild Adventure Specials
programme.desc: In the first of two programmes in which two sports stars test their survival instincts, former rugby union player Jonny Wilkinson joins Bear Grylls to explore Dartmoor's harsh and gruelling terrain, an adventure that will push his physical and mental limits. From climbing 40 metres up a sheer cliff face to crossing ice cold rivers, Jonny also learns the key basic survival skills necessary to survive in remote terrain like Dartmoor

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: Lingo
programme.desc: Adil Ray invites three pairs of contestants to call upon their powers of deduction in a battle of words. Using their powers of deduction, they must correctly and quickly work out the words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords. The last remaining duo have the opportunity to win the money they've banked earlier in the show, potentially doubling their winnings or leaving with nothing. First up are father and son Simon and Will, competition fanatic Sue and husband Paul, and mother and daughter Dawn and Regan

programme.desc: The gang learns of even bigger and even worse forces working against the Empress and the Kingdom of China and must come up with a plan to put a stop to them before it is too late.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Dave is warned by local fortune teller Madam Donald that walking under a ladder will bring bad luck - he walks under one anyway, and soon a meteor is heading for Grimsby. It is up to Dave and his friend Robert Robot to catch it before everyone is squished.

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James serves up a feast of food for This Morning's resident medic Ranj Singh, including recipes for risotto, lemon sole, salmon en croute and rhubarb crumble cake. Plus, chefs Sat Bains and Bettina Campolucci Bordi drop by with delicious dishes of their own

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef shares his love of new produce from the gardens and explains why seasonality is important and why everyone should embrace it. From his Oxfordshire kitchen, he cooks pan-fried salmon with spinach and chive butter sauce, and rustles up a fricassee of wild mushrooms. Long-time friend Jean-Christophe Novelli pays a visit and bakes him an indulgent tarte tatin, and baked apple with caramel

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef visits the Languedoc region, famous for its wine and the hearty, meaty dish cassoulet, and stops at a roadside fruit stall owned by a couple of British expats, before creating an unusual dish using melon and onion rings. He also wanders round a museum dedicated to Citroen 2CVs and cooks a steak on a barbecue in the blazing sunshine

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Bullard asks Barnaby to investigate the disappearance of a friend's daughter who was a member of the Oblong Foundation, a cult based at Malham Hall. Jones goes under cover as a new recruit to solve the mystery, but he and Barnaby soon start digging into an old case involving the death of a couple in a boat explosion, which could be linked to the missing woman. Detective drama, starring Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes, with Will Knightley, Kevin Doyle, Holly Aird, Barbara Flynn and John Woodvine

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: Ireland v England (Kick-off 4.45pm). Coverage of both teams' concluding match of the championship, held at Aviva Stadium. England prevailed 24-12 when the sides met at Twickenham last year and also won 32-20 here in 2019, but they have made an unconvincing defence of their title and Ireland will fancy their chances on this occasion despite also enduring a far from flawless campaign. Mark Pougatch presents, with analysis from Jonny Wilkinson, Clive Woodward, Brian O'Driscoll and Rory Best, and commentary by Nick Mullins, Lawrence Dallaglio and Geordan Murphy

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Saturday Night Takeaway
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan is in the guest announcer booth, while Cat Deeley is in the studio for one of the riskiest Undercovers ever as Ant and Dec wreak havoc on their former SMTV Live co-star. After 20 years, the trio also reunite with a whole host of celebrity pals for the return of their much-loved sitcom Chums. Plus, Stephen Mulhern is back for another sporting challenge and a member of the virtual audience gets the chance to Win the Ads

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis presents the climax of the spinning-chair singing contest as the last four acts battle it out for the chance to win a recording contract, with coaches Tom Jones, Anne-Marie, Olly Murs and Will.i.am looking on

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show: Special Guests
programme.desc: Another selection of the most memorable moments from recent series of the talk show. Among the guests featured are David Attenborough, Sheridan Smith, Kevin Hart, Michael Caine, Julie Walters, Hugh Jackman, Liam Gallagher, Michelle Keegan, Ed Sheeran, Tiffany Haddish and Robbie Williams.

programme.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day (1991)
programme.desc: A reprogrammed killing machine is sent back in time to the 20th century to protect John Connor, a teenage boy whose destiny is to save the human race. After breaking John's mother out of a mental institution, the pair set out to avert a future nuclear war - pursued by an advanced prototype terminator with shape-changing powers. Sci-fi action adventure sequel, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Linda Hamilton, Edward Furlong and Robert Patrick

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Lady Penelope and Parker are testing out FAB2, a luxury yacht, when they receive a distress call from a nearby tanker. A Tsunami has erupted, and a massive wave is headed for the stalled tanker's location

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The police suspect Bean of stealing his landlady's jewellery - but the real culprit is a magpie he's been nursing back to health

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series

programme.title: Mighty Mike

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: An immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Anna is training to be a magician with Dave as her helpful assistant, but their disappearing trick is not up to much - that is, until Zombie Katie bestows a magic spell book upon them. Soon they can't stop making stuff disappear, but what will happen when there is nothing left to make disappear in Grimsby?

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: At 694 metres in height, Pen-y-ghent is the smallest fell on the Yorkshire Three Peaks Challenge, but the dramatic summit affords far-reaching views above the Dales, with plenty of surprises en route. The peak lies 3km east of Horton in Ribblesdale and the Pennine Way links the summit to the village. As Julia reveals, the route is around 5km in length, as the Way curves initially to the north before turning east to reach the summit

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan welcomes former boxer Frank Bruno and actor Stephen Mangan, while David Domoney has the perfect fruit trees for any patch, and there is a selection of the best British fruity tipples

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef shares his love of new produce from the gardens and explains why seasonality is important and why everyone should embrace it. From his Oxfordshire kitchen, he cooks pan-fried salmon with spinach and chive butter sauce, and rustles up a fricassee of wild mushrooms. Long-time friend Jean-Christophe Novelli pays a visit and bakes him an indulgent tarte tatin, and baked apple with caramel

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team create a garden for a family with twin girls who have a serious, life-limiting condition. Three-year-olds Darcie and Evie Chapman have been diagnosed with Dravet syndrome, a very rare form of epilepsy, and parents Mark and Natalie must be on permanent watch to prevent their daughters' seizures leading to something more serious. The Chapmans' current outdoor space is unsuitable for the girls, with changes of levels, colours of plants and even the light through branches influencing their condition, so the crew follows the rules of a formal garden where straight lines, order and symmetry give an unfussy overall look

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec present a selection of memorable, surprising and extraordinary auditions from the various editions of the talent show franchise from around the world, including those of X, Callum Scott and Kseniya Simonova

programme.title: Minions (2015)
programme.desc: A race of strange, babbling yellow creatures has served the cause of evil throughout the history of the world. In the 1960s, three of these eternal henchmen get a job working for a criminal genius who is hatching a plot against the British crown - but while carrying out her orders, they uncover a threat to their whole species. Animated comedy, with the voices of Sandra Bullock, Pierre Coffin, Jon Hamm and Michael Keaton

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Compilation of some of the most dramatic endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Susan Calman, Dermot Murnaghan, Perri Shakes-Drayton and Omid Djalili pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: All Star Musicals
programme.desc: John Barrowman hosts a show as celebrities Ranj Singh, Robert Peston, Rob Rinder, Barney Walsh, Jessica Hynes and Luisa Bradshaw-White perform songs from hit musicals such as Chicago, The Greatest Showman, Guys and Dolls and Beauty & the Beast. They will be trying to impress musical theatre stars Elaine Paige, Samantha Barks and Trevor Dion Nicholas, as well as the viewing audience

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local aristocrats and beekeepers the Deddingtons have built up a thriving empire, producing a honey with extraordinary healing power that has put the village of Granville Norton on the map. But how far will they go to ensure they are never dethroned? Griff Rhys Jones and Imogen Stubbs guest star in the mystery drama, with Neil Dudgeon and Nick Hendrix

programme.title: DNA Journey
programme.desc: Britain's Got Talent judge Amanda Holden and comedian Alan Carr embark on journeys across the country as they delve into their family histories, unlocking secrets of the past and meeting close family they never knew existed. Amanda's journey starts in the grounds of a stately home in Cornwall, while Alan's hunt for his ancestors takes him to a pub in Erith with something of a colourful past

programme.title: Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Ugo Monye presents action from the fifth round of fixtures, featuring France v Wales at Stade de France, Ireland v England at Aviva Stadium and Scotland v Italy at BT Murrayfield. With analysis from Maggie Alphonsi and Gareth Thomas

programme.title: 2020: The Story of Us
programme.desc: Oscar-winning director Kevin Macdonald's feature-length documentary tells the story of coronavirus in Britain through the experience of people immersed in its impact, both personally and professionally. Using testimony from staff in hospitals - consultants, doctors and nurses - as well as weaving in the experience of patients, this documentary paints a vivid portrait of the working lives of ICU staff and those they treat during a crisis which proves both challenging, life-changing and often tragic for those caught in it

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team are in Dorset creating an exciting garden for a young family. His idea for the Drakes is to give them an outdoor space that reflects terminally ill dad Andrew's love of the great outdoors and his passion for working with wood. He opts for a garden with a frontier-land theme that Andrew, wife Rachel and their young son Lucas can enjoy, creating a prairie-inspired back yard of big scale rustic wood structures, agricultural motifs with craftsmanship and the spirit of adventure at its core

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: While Fiz is humiliated, Tyrone admits he has fallen for Alina. Elsewhere, Leanne stashes her burner phone before telling Harvey she wants out, and Daisy offers to help Sean out, at a price

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Coleen Nolan chats to Piers Morgan about her life in the spotlight, from her first stage appearance, in Blackpool, at the age of two, to selling more than 25 million records with the Nolans. The Loose Women star also opens up about the cancer 'curse' that has devastated her family, and reveals that she is considering a mastectomy after seeing three of her sisters battle the disease, including Bernie, who passed away in 2013

programme.title: Bear Grylls Wild Adventure Specials
programme.desc: In the first of two programmes in which two sports stars test their survival instincts, former rugby union player Jonny Wilkinson joins Bear Grylls to explore Dartmoor's harsh and gruelling terrain, an adventure that will push his physical and mental limits. From climbing 40 metres up a sheer cliff face to crossing ice cold rivers, Jonny also learns the key basic survival skills necessary to survive in remote terrain like Dartmoor

programme.title: Take Me Out

programme.title: Happy Feet (2006)
programme.desc: A young penguin disappoints his fellow seabirds with his inability to sing - but instead he displays a remarkable talent for tap dancing. Animated adventure, with the voices of Elijah Wood, Robin Williams, Nicole Kidman and Hugh Jackman

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: ITV2 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin is struggling to live off his student grant, so the girls find a way for him to earn hard cash. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Harvey threatens to put Leanne and Simon in hospital unless he gets all of the money he is owed. Tyrone flirts with Alina in the cafe and it's clear she enjoys his attention. Sally insists on helping Abi plan her wedding and appoints herself Maid of Honour

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective's curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. When the vessel arrives at Alexandria, the irritating lady's body is discovered in her cabin - leaving the sleuth with a host of possible suspects to interrogate. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The members of an Oxford old boys' cricket team gather for their annual match, unaware that death is waiting at the crease to bowl one of them out for good. Morse sets out to find the culprit - helped as ever by the faithful Lewis, who demonstrates unexpected skill on the pitch. John Thaw, Kevin Whately and Sharon Maughan star in an episode scripted by Oscar-winning director Anthony Minghella

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A diary and a parking ticket are found at a murder scene in a multi-storey car park. It is not much to go on, but Morse soon links the clues to a crime of passion involving a jealous husband, his wife and her lover. On the personal front, the Oxford detective's feathers are ruffled by a woman he finds himself attracted to. John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Mel Martin and Amanda Hillwood star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Businessman and owner of the Causton Lions rugby team Mark Adler is mysteriously found dead in the club's cryotherapy chamber, which is normally used by the players for physiotherapy. Barnaby and Winter are called in to investigate and their list of suspects soon includes Adler's wife Samantha, former coach Guy Bevan, troubled star player Danny Wickham and physio Neville Gallagher. Neil Dudgeon stars, with Tamzin Outhwaite, Don Gilet and Michael Maloney

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When Barnaby is dragged along to the wedding of the son of Sarah's friend Hazel, the last thing he expects to find himself doing is investigating a murder. But when his wife discovers the body of the bride, Laurel, who has been squeezed to death by her own wedding dress corset, the detective must look more closely at his fellow guests. The victim's husband Gavin is convinced that his wife's death had everything to do with her outspoken nature on her radio show, so Winter's attention soon turns to her unique community of nocturnal listeners. Gavin's scorned ex-fiancee and local wedding dress designer Serena also comes under suspicion, and Barnaby and Winter's enquiries lead them to a local wedding fayre. Neil Dudgeon stars, with Kelly Brook, Nick Hancock and Liz Fraser

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Things take a gruesome turn when Ferabbees Circus comes to town, bringing with it a chain of sinister clown sightings, threatening notes and deathly dangerous circus acts. Barnaby has to face up to his fears in order to solve the case. Drama, starring Neil Dudgeon, Nick Hendrix, Lorraine Ashbourne and Annette Badland

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: In a retrospective episode set immediately after the events of series two, Rachel faces further accusations of masterminding the fatal attack on ex-lover Nick and finds comfort in the arms of ever-faithful Sean, agreeing to marry him. Amid the wedding celebrations, Janet and Ade admit defeat and decide to split up for good, while Gill is duty bound to take action when she catches Pete in a compromising position. The detectives also investigate the case of a businessman suspected of murdering a homeless man

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the murder of a wealthy man whose double life left him vulnerable to exploitation. Away from work, Rachel's recent moment of indiscretion threatens to come back to haunt her when a confused Sean takes a phone call from her one-night stand, and after Ade is pushed into living with his girlfriend on a permanent basis, Janet asks her mother to move in

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Unexplained killings rock the Bodmin community, giving rise to fears that the so-called 'beast', a monster enshrined in local legend, has returned to wreak fresh havoc. Kersey ventures up onto the moors to investigate and does not return - so Wycliffe is forced to go it alone. Jack Shepherd and Jimmy Yuill star

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The flatmates come up with a new rule to give their romantic pursuits a chance of privacy - but it means giving 24 hours' notice. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Harvey threatens to put Leanne and Simon in hospital unless he gets all of the money he is owed. Tyrone flirts with Alina in the cafe and it's clear she enjoys his attention. Sally insists on helping Abi plan her wedding and appoints herself Maid of Honour

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Will covers for Harriet's absence. Faith is suspicious of Mack. Kim gloats about her meddling

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective finds himself investigating the strange and gruesome murder of an elderly psychiatrist. Elsewhere, Poirot's old friend Ariadne Oliver is being pressed to uncover the truth behind two decade-old deaths. As the pair dig deep for evidence, they soon discover that their separate investigations are linked and join forces to solve the overarching mystery. David Suchet, Zoe Wanamaker and Greta Scacchi star

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A highly respected scientist is murdered just before he is about to deliver a controversial lecture on the environment. It becomes clear he had many enemies within the profession, but Morse's investigations lead him to a killer who is altogether closer to home. Mystery, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Geoffrey Palmer, Cheryl Campbell and George Costigan

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Called to investigate the murder of a brewery manager, Morse delves into the history of the generations-old family firm and discovers that several employees and shareholders stood to profit from the victim's death. The challenge facing him is to discern whether any of them would stoop to foul play. Guest starring Lionel Jeffries and John Bird

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell investigate the case of Maria Roberts, the daughter of Italian parents who had a happy adoption growing up with an English family, and now hopes to find Gilda, her birth mother. The team also meets Francesca Barnes, who is in the early stages of transitioning from male to female. She was born with the name Paul and given up for adoption as a baby. Francesca is now searching for her mother, Norma, but fears her transition may prove too much of a challenge, and lead to rejection

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Japan
programme.desc: The actress heads to the island of Shikoku hoping to gain a better understanding of Japanese Buddhism. She then takes a bullet train to another island - Kyushu - where she finds the Henn Na Hotel, the world's first robot hotel. At Nagasaki, she visits Shiroyama Elementary school, one of the only buildings to survive the atomic bomb dropped on the city in 1945. Joanna then travels to Sakurajima, one of the country's most active volcanoes, before heading to the islands of Okinawa where one of the bloodiest battles of the Second World War was fought

programme.title: The More You Ignore Me (2018)
programme.desc: During the 1980s, a Morrissey-obsessed teenager finds her life regularly derailed by her mother's erratic mental state. Comedy, starring Sheridan Smith, Ella Hunt, Mark Addy and Jo Brand, who also adapted the screenplay from her 2009 novel of the same name

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Troubled saleswoman Helen (Nicola Walker), whose mother was murdered back in episode one, turns up on Janet's doorstep with information that takes the investigation in a completely different direction - paving the way for the formidable Det Supt Julie Dodson to be called in. Meanwhile, new recruit Sgt Rob Waddington arrives on the scene - putting Janet's nose out of joint as she is demoted back down to constable. Emmerdale star Danny Miller (Aaron Livesy) joins the cast

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Having realised her marriage is a mistake, Rachel is still staying at Janet's and making excuses for her absence, but Sean isn't prepared to accept that things have gone so wrong, so quickly. Meanwhile, the investigation into Joe Bevan continues as the body count rises and the team faces the mammoth task of identifying the victims and determining the order in which they were murdered. Detective drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp and Sean Maguire

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Dorothy becomes concerned her grand-daughter Taisie is being influenced by Rachel, while Janet is mortified when Gill berates her for inappropriate behaviour toward young sergeant Rob. The detective duo are sent to investigate the death of a man in a nursing home after his daughter complains about the level of care he had been receiving. Will they find any evidence of foul play? Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp, Suranne Jones and Danny Miller

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A wartime memento prompts Sid and Jean to visit Paris. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Will covers for Harriet's absence. Faith is suspicious of Mack. Kim gloats about her meddling

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fred thinks Ashley is a fool for letting Zoe stay with him. Deirdre decides to surprise Jon at work. Chris is pleased when Kevin offers him his job back

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz is stunned when Deirdre tells her about Jon's wife and children. Emily enjoys having Spider live with her. Nick and Leanne leave for Scotland together

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris remains unconscious outside Home Farm. Rachel and Graham make up and he lies to her about the night before. The jury deliver their verdicts in Kim and Steve's court case

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Viv wants Terry to help out at the post office, but Kelly is against the idea. Seth receives a bottle of brandy from Kim. Zoe gives Chris a piece of her mind

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick and Kate must act quickly when a woman takes an overdose, in the belief that her husband has been falsely imprisoned. Meanwhile, Phil learns Jennifer is pregnant and agrees to marry her, but may not survive his stag night at the pub. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry, Niamh Cusack and Mark Jordan

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sally and Mike enter the junk market and use the garden to store their wares - until Sid puts his foot down. Comedy, starring Sally Geeson and Robin Stewart

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan has a go at installing a new toilet, but soon discovers some jobs are best left to the experts

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: A female trainee arrives at the depot and attracts the attention of the men - until they discover she is the inspector's niece

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with old friend Ariadne Oliver, who has been commissioned to stage a game of murder hunt at a summer fete in the grounds of Nasse House, the recently purchased home of wealthy financier Sir George Stubbs. However, the novelist's instincts tell her she's getting involved in something much darker - and she's proved right when the girl volunteering to play the part of the victim really does turn out to be dead. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Zoe Wanamaker, Sean Pertwee, Martin Jarvis, Sam Kelly, Sinead Cusack, Rosalind Ayres and Tom Ellis

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Kate's ex-boyfriend turns up in Aidensfield and finds himself under suspicion for a hit-and-run accident. Meanwhile, Bellamy finds himself in charge of organising a local talent contest. Rural police drama, starring Niamh Cusack and Mark Jordon

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy urges Chris to leave the past behind. Jack has bad news from Germany about Tony Cairns. Dawn asks Ned to go to Ibiza with her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Turner decides to take on a new manager and Tricia fancies her chances. Zoe pours out her troubles to Paddy. Lisa and Zak argue about going straight

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre is annoyed at herself for not realising Jon was already married. Gail is horrified when she learns Leanne is with Nick in Scotland

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz starts works a trainee machinist at Underworld, and Mike assures Alma he does not fancy the new recruit. Nick and Leanne decide to get married, and Gail tries to hunt him down

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A rehearsal of an amateur operatic production ends with Morse being arrested on suspicion of murder and suspended from duty pending further investigation. Stripped of official powers, he fights to clear his name - unaware he has been pencilled in as the real killer's next target. Guest starring Ian McDiarmid, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls victim to depression after failing to prevent the murder of a society girl by notorious art thief Marrascaud. His confidence shattered, Poirot eventually returns to work when a lonely chauffeur begs him to find his missing soulmate, the maid of a famous Russian dancer. The investigation takes him to the Swiss Alps, where, against all odds, he stumbles upon a hotel thought to be Marrascaud's hideout - leading to a very personal showdown. The penultimate episode of the long-running detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Simon Callow, Orla Brady, Patrick Ryecart, Nigel Lindsay, Morven Christie, Tom Austen and Rupert Evans

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Julia McKenzie returns as the sleuthing spinster in the first of three murder mysteries. While staying at a hotel in the Caribbean, Miss Marple investigates the death of a fellow guest, refusing to believe he died of a heart attack. With the help of a business tycoon she sets about finding the real cause of the man's demise, and is soon drawn into a web of deceit and dark magic. Adapted by Charlie Higson and co-starring Antony Sher, Robert Webb, Montserrat Lombard, Daniel Rigby, Hermione Norris, MyAnna Buring, Warren Brown and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The evacuation of a central London hospital leads to the discovery of a tragic death. When Brooks and Devlin investigate, a suspect is arrested but the prosecutors (Ben Daniels and Freema Agyeman) face an uphill struggle building a case - so they team up with the detectives in a bid to gather more evidence. Patrick Malahide and Bill Paterson co-star

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: With their friendship in tatters, the lack of communication between Rachel and Janet is making even the simplest of tasks difficult, and it takes a call about Gill's possible abduction to reunite them as a team. The duo soon have Gill's car in their sights and identify the kidnapper, but discover they are heading for a notorious suicide location. Can the detectives get to their boss in time?

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A rehearsal of an amateur operatic production ends with Morse being arrested on suspicion of murder and suspended from duty pending further investigation. Stripped of official powers, he fights to clear his name - unaware he has been pencilled in as the real killer's next target. Guest starring Ian McDiarmid, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A rehearsal of an amateur operatic production ends with Morse being arrested on suspicion of murder and suspended from duty pending further investigation. Stripped of official powers, he fights to clear his name - unaware he has been pencilled in as the real killer's next target. Guest starring Ian McDiarmid, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth falls victim to depression after failing to prevent the murder of a society girl by notorious art thief Marrascaud. His confidence shattered, Poirot eventually returns to work when a lonely chauffeur begs him to find his missing soulmate, the maid of a famous Russian dancer. The investigation takes him to the Swiss Alps, where, against all odds, he stumbles upon a hotel thought to be Marrascaud's hideout - leading to a very personal showdown. The penultimate episode of the long-running detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Simon Callow, Orla Brady, Patrick Ryecart, Nigel Lindsay, Morven Christie, Tom Austen and Rupert Evans

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Julia McKenzie returns as the sleuthing spinster in the first of three murder mysteries. While staying at a hotel in the Caribbean, Miss Marple investigates the death of a fellow guest, refusing to believe he died of a heart attack. With the help of a business tycoon she sets about finding the real cause of the man's demise, and is soon drawn into a web of deceit and dark magic. Adapted by Charlie Higson and co-starring Antony Sher, Robert Webb, Montserrat Lombard, Daniel Rigby, Hermione Norris, MyAnna Buring, Warren Brown and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The evacuation of a central London hospital leads to the discovery of a tragic death. When Brooks and Devlin investigate, a suspect is arrested but the prosecutors (Ben Daniels and Freema Agyeman) face an uphill struggle building a case - so they team up with the detectives in a bid to gather more evidence. Patrick Malahide and Bill Paterson co-star

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: With their friendship in tatters, the lack of communication between Rachel and Janet is making even the simplest of tasks difficult, and it takes a call about Gill's possible abduction to reunite them as a team. The duo soon have Gill's car in their sights and identify the kidnapper, but discover they are heading for a notorious suicide location. Can the detectives get to their boss in time?

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A rehearsal of an amateur operatic production ends with Morse being arrested on suspicion of murder and suspended from duty pending further investigation. Stripped of official powers, he fights to clear his name - unaware he has been pencilled in as the real killer's next target. Guest starring Ian McDiarmid, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: A popular singer fears for her sanity when she starts being terrorised by ghosts. Drama, starring Robert Vaughn, Nyree Dawn Porter and Eartha Kitt

programme.title: The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A reclusive lodger's strange behaviour sends his landlady running to Holmes for advice - and what initially seems to be a simple matter turns into a mystery involving an Italian secret society and murder in New York. Drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: Hornblower
programme.desc: First of a two-part drama, which sees CS Forester's seafaring hero crossing swords with tyrannical Captain James Sawyer, an officer on the brink of madness. Reprimanded for falling asleep while on watch, young Horatio partly redeems himself by saving the Renown from seemingly certain disaster _ but ends up charged with mutiny. Ioan Gruffudd, Robert Lindsay and David Warner star

programme.title: ITV Racing: The Opening Show
programme.desc: Rishi Persad presents a look ahead to the day's racing at Uttoxeter and a review of Cheltenham Festival

programme.title: The Big Match Revisited
programme.desc: Brian Moore presents action from November 1979, including Liverpool, Arsenal, Everton v Tottenham Hotspur and Notts County v Chelsea

programme.title: Euro 2020 Preview Show
programme.desc: The look ahead to the long-awaited tournament continues with profiles of Italy and Scotland, plus a look back at a special moment from 1988

programme.title: ITV Football Classics
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces extended highlights of the quarter-final match from Stade Pierre-Mauroy in Lille, France. Chris Coleman's side had impressed many to reach the knockout rounds, with Gareth Bale and Aaron Ramsey in starring roles, but it was their less-celebrated teammates who grabbed the headlines against the highly-fancied Belgians in this entertaining match. Commentary by Clive Tyldesley and Craig Bellamy

programme.title: Sport Filler Shows (2020)
programme.desc: A look back at memorable stagings of the race from Epsom Downs. Featuring brilliant performances from Shergar to Sea The Stars, plus interviews with personalities from the worlds of sport and entertainment

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of four races from Uttoxeter, including the 3.35 Marston's 61 Deep Midlands Grand National, and three races from Kempton Park. With analysis by Andrew Thornton and Luke Harvey, betting news from Rishi Persad, commentary by Richard Hoiles and reports from Natalie Green

programme.title: The Sea Wolves (1980)
programme.desc: The German Navy use three Goa-based vessels to attack Allied shipping, and their British counterparts cannot retaliate as they are moored in neutral waters. A group of retired officers living in India unofficially returns to duty to deal with the threat. Fact-based Second World War adventure, starring Gregory Peck, Roger Moore, David Niven, Trevor Howard, Barbara Kellerman and Patrick Macnee

programme.title: The Magnificent Seven (1960)
programme.desc: Oppressed Mexican villagers cross the border into America looking to buy guns to defend their homes and crops from bandits. Instead they encounter an enigmatic loner who persuades them to hire a band of gunslingers to teach the villagers how to fight before the marauders return. Western, with Yul Brynner, Steve McQueen, Charles Bronson, Robert Vaughn, James Coburn and Eli Wallach

Western, directed by and starring Clint Eastwood, alongside Verna Bloom and Marianna Hill

programme.title: The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes

programme.title: Hornblower
programme.desc: Hornblower's resourcefulness turns the attack on the Spanish-held fortress into a famous victory, but Buckland's treachery puts his life into further danger. Surviving a risky mission to blow up the fortifications and an attempt by the prisoners to take command of the Renown, the hero faces the death penalty for pushing Captain Sawyer into the hold - and hopes Archie's dying words can save him from the noose. Naval adventure, based on the stories by CS Forester, and starring Ioan Gruffudd

programme.title: Grand Slam Years
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke presents a look back at a memorable year for Irish rugby as Joe Schmidt's side secured only the third Grand Slam in Ireland's history. Highlights included Johnny Sexton's last-minute drop goal against France and an emphatic victory over England at Twickenham on St Patrick's Day

programme.title: Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Ugo Monye presents action from the fifth round of fixtures, featuring France v Wales at Stade de France, Ireland v England at Aviva Stadium and Scotland v Italy at BT Murrayfield. With analysis from Maggie Alphonsi and Gareth Thomas

programme.title: Step Brothers (2008)
programme.desc: Brennan and Dale are two 40-year-old men who have never flown the nest. All that changes when Brennan's mother marries Dale's father, and the spoiled sons are forced to share a room. They take an instant dislike to each other, but when their long-suffering parents finally insist they find jobs, they learn to work together. Comedy, with Will Ferrell, John C Reilly, Mary Steenburgen and Richard Jenkins

Fantasy adventure, based on the stories by author Robert E Howard, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Max von Sydow, James Earl Jones and Sandahl Bergman

programme.title: World of Sport
programme.desc: Richard Henwood looks back at television sporting firsts, including footage of a 14-year-old Ronnie O'Sullivan who would go on to take snooker by storm

programme.title: The Big Fish Off
programme.desc: Team captains Ali Hamidi and Dean Macey head to Thailand with Towie star Ricky Rayment and super-featherweight boxer Mitchell Smith, targeting Mekong catfish, Siamese carp and arapaima

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Nightclub owner Paul Verrier is tricked into believing that the Saint is trying to kill him, so contacts various people to murder Templer. One of his nefarious contacts is unknown to him visually, so when the Saint eventually pays a call Verrier mistakes him for his hired assassin and inadvertently hires the Saint to kill himself. Roger Moore stars

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A series of robberies is committed on affluent couples travelling down a country lane, and it transpires two of the suspected offenders are wealthy themselves. Lesley-Anne Down guest stars alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed's friends, Bill and Laura Basset, find themselves in trouble when some unwelcome visitors turn up at their house. Luckily, Steed is also planning a visit. Patrick Macnee and Linda Thorson star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A rival dealer schemes to put Arthur out of business, forcing him to advertise on pirate radio in a bid to stay afloat. George Cole and Gary Webster star

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: The secret of producing a classic Drumstick lolly at Britain's biggest family-owned sweet factory, and the making of guitars in demand from the likes of Dolly Parton and Ed Sheeran. Plus, how a roll of steel is turned into a finished radiator in a matter of minutes, and a company combining robotics and traditional artistry to create shotguns

programme.title: Snooker: Tour Championship Live
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage of the afternoon session on day one at Celtic Manor resort in Newport, Wales, featuring the first eight frames of the opening quarter-final. With analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Ken Doherty, Neal Foulds and Rob Walker, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade investigates the stories behind supposedly deadly freshwater creatures, beginning by examining whether piranhas deserve their fearsome reputation. He travels to Brazil to swim in the Amazon with the fish and incites them into a feeding frenzy, before tracking down survivors and eyewitnesses of attacks

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A car he sold in 1970 returns to haunt Arthur - who ends up being framed for crimes he did not commit. Drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: How a homemade guitar amp changed the course of music history overnight and how a team of expert pen makers are giving a Second World War fighter a new lease of life

programme.title: Sport Filler Shows (2020)
programme.desc: Great moments from the sporting archives.

programme.title: Snooker: Tour Championship Live
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage of the evening session on day one at Celtic Manor resort in Newport, Wales, featuring the conclusion of the opening quarter-final. The top eight snooker players on the one-year list will compete for a £150,000 first prize and the title won by Stephen Maguire in Milton Keynes last year. Maguire has not qualified to defend his title, but the likes of Judd Trump, Mark Selby, Neil Robertson, John Higgins and Ronnie O'Sullivan will be present. With analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Ken Doherty, Neal Foulds and Rob Walker, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

programme.title: Crank (2006)
programme.desc: An assassin faces a race against time when he learns a rival has poisoned him with a drug that will kill him if his heart rate drops below a certain level. As he sets out to take revenge on his enemies, he has to resort to increasingly desperate acts to keep his adrenaline pumping. Action thriller, with Jason Statham, Amy Smart and Jose Pablo Cantillo

Fact-based Second World War adventure, starring Gregory Peck, Roger Moore, David Niven, Trevor Howard, Barbara Kellerman and Patrick Macnee

programme.title: Hornblower
programme.desc: Hornblower's resourcefulness turns the attack on the Spanish-held fortress into a famous victory, but Buckland's treachery puts his life into further danger. Surviving a risky mission to blow up the fortifications and an attempt by the prisoners to take command of the Renown, the hero faces the death penalty for pushing Captain Sawyer into the hold - and hopes Archie's dying words can save him from the noose. Naval adventure, based on the stories by CS Forester, and starring Ioan Gruffudd

programme.title: Grand Slam Years
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke presents a look back at a memorable year for Irish rugby as Joe Schmidt's side secured only the third Grand Slam in Ireland's history. Highlights included Johnny Sexton's last-minute drop goal against France and an emphatic victory over England at Twickenham on St Patrick's Day

programme.title: Six Nations Highlights

programme.title: Step Brothers (2008)
programme.desc: Brennan and Dale are two 40-year-old men who have never flown the nest. All that changes when Brennan's mother marries Dale's father, and the spoiled sons are forced to share a room. They take an instant dislike to each other, but when their long-suffering parents finally insist they find jobs, they learn to work together. Comedy, with Will Ferrell, John C Reilly, Mary Steenburgen and Richard Jenkins
